what: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals
title: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals
text: end--end toolkit factor investing research deployment: engineer point--time signals, tame factor zoo disciplined multiple-testing inference, blend signals machine learning, build cost- constraint-aware portfolios, one consistent, auditable interface. factoRverse takes factor strategy raw firm-level data way deployable portfolio. packages modern asset-pricing workflow (clean realistic anomaly construction, multiple-testing / factor-zoo debate, signal blending Machine-Learning classical way, multiple --sample robust portfolio construction methods) small, composable, testable functions, strict guardrails two cardinal sins backtesting: look-ahead bias survivorship bias. built around metabackesting idea: point time equity factor investor must decide signals use, process, select combine . Therefore, package helps experimenting full deployable end--end systems, instead simple backtests. providing modular functions S4 objects, needed steps developing quantitative multifactor strategy easy implement test: feature engineering, data pre-processing, portfolio construction, strategy selection signal blending (including Machine-Learning Meta-Ensembling). Everything already built financial time series data, walk-forward point--time manner.

title: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals
text: Databricks frames MLOps DevOps + DataOps + ModelOps, factoRverse adds PortOps: deployment portfolios given signals developed models, calls whole thing FactorOps. practice means package built reproducibility auditing, just computation: Every artifact carries history. core S4 objects (meta_dataframe, meta_xts, *_results objects) store workflow slot recording exactly steps produced , table backtest can traced back inputs. Configuration modular swappable. workflow driven config object build fluent create_*_config() |> add_*() pipeline (e.g. ss_backtest_config carries alpha_test_strategy, may carry bayesian_model_parameters). Pieces snap together can reused across runs. Runs self-identifying. Named configs propagate backtest_identifier every *_results object, experiments stay labelled comparable. Two naming conventions recur throughout: objects suffixed _m_df meta_dataframes (long panels) _m_xts meta_xts (wide time series); every workflow follows shape: config + data objects, run_*(), *_results object.

title: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals
code: flowchart TD     RAW[Raw firm-level data] -->|create_meta_dataframe / create_meta_xts| BRONZE[Bronze]     BRONZE -->|compute_ fns, map_recipe_timewise| SILVER[Silver]     SILVER --> GOLD[Gold features_m_df]      GOLD -->|create_target_m_df| TGT[target_m_df]      GOLD --> W1     subgraph W1 [1 Characteristic portfolios]         PC["create_port_backtest_config + add_*"] --> RPB[run_port_backtest] --> PBR[port_backtest_results]         PBR --> COH[create_port_backtest_cohort]     end      COH --> W2     subgraph W2 [2 Signal selection]         SC["create_ss_backtest_config + add_alpha_test_strategy"] --> RSS[run_ss_backtest] --> SSR[ss_backtest_results]     end      SSR --> W3     TGT --> W3     subgraph W3 [3 Signal blending]         SBC["create_sb_backtest_config + add_tuning_strategy"] --> RSB[run_sb_backtest] --> SBR[sb_backtest_results]     end      SBR -->|per-stock exp_ret_score| W4     subgraph W4 [4 Deployed portfolio]         RPB2[run_port_backtest with sb_backtest_results] --> FIN[Final book]     end

previous_headings: A tour of the four workflows
title: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals
text: Wrap panel validated, self-documenting container, engineer signals factor-zoo literature, preprocess one date time future information leaks past. tickers_catalog (stable, rename-proof perm_ids listing/delisting bookkeeping) create_target_m_df() (dozens forward, risk-adjusted targets) round data layer.
code: library(factoRverse)  gold_m_df <- create_meta_dataframe(raw_panel, meta_dataframe_name = "features")  gold_m_df <- gold_m_df |>   compute_formula(book_yield ~ book_equity / market_cap) |>   compute_window(period = 12, FUN = "res_mom", signal = "ret",     # residual momentum                  benchmark_returns_m_xts = ibov_m_xts, selected_bench = "ibov") |>   compute_sector_wise(sector_column = "sector", signal = "net_margin", FUN = "median")  # Per-date recipe: sector-mean imputation + winsorization, prepped/baked date by date rec <- recipes::recipe(gold_m_df@data) |>   recipes::update_role(id, tickers, dates, new_role = "id_vars") |>   recipes::update_role(recipes::all_numeric(), new_role = "predictor") |>   step_impute_sector(recipes::all_numeric_predictors(), sector = "sector") |>   step_winsorize(recipes::all_numeric_predictors(), probs = c(0.025, 0.975))  gold_m_df <- map_recipe_timewise(gold_m_df, rec, type = "signals")

previous_headings: A tour of the four workflows
title: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals
text: Rank stocks signal, invest top quantile, backtest resulting long-portfolio realistic liquidity filters transaction costs.
code: value_config <- create_port_backtest_config(   chosen_score_metric_and_position = c(book_yield = "long"),   eligibility_quantile_range = c(0.9, 1),          # top decile   selected_benchmark = "ibov",   initial_buffer_period = 12, rebalancing_months = c(6, 12),   main_liquidity_metric = "mean_volfin_3m",   port_construction_method = "ew",                 # ew/sw/cw/cs/rp/hrp/mvo/mmaf   config_name = "ew_book_yield" ) |>   add_liquidity_constraint_policy(liquidity_floor_rule = "micro_caps") |>   add_transaction_costs_parameters(direct_transaction_cost = 0.07,                                    alpha = 0.5, lambda = "dynamic",                                    strategy_aum = 2e7)  value_bt <- run_port_backtest(   signals_m_df    = gold_m_df,   fwd_return_m_df = fwd_1m_returns_m_df,   liquidity_m_df  = liquidity_m_df,   volatility_m_df = volatility_m_df,   config          = value_config )                                                  # -> port_backtest_results  # Aggregate many style portfolios and compare them as a group cohort <- create_port_backtest_cohort(   list(value_bt, momentum_bt, quality_bt), cohort_name = "styles" ) summary(cohort); plot(cohort)

previous_headings: A tour of the four workflows
what: 2. Signal selection: controlling the factor zoo
title: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals
text: signals real? Pass backtested returns candidate portfolios disciplined, walk-forward inference CAPM alpha, frequentist multiple-testing control (FWER: Bonferroni/Holm; FDR: BH/) Bayesian hierarchical shrinkage partially pools alphas within economically motivated themes. workflow studied Factor Zoo Revisited (see ). Bayesian route, set p_correction_method = "bayesian" attach add_bayesian_model_parameters(). Priors can user-defined estimated exogenous global dataset.
code: ss_config <- create_ss_backtest_config(   initial_sample_size = 80, rebalancing_months = 6,   chosen_signals_and_positions = "all", split_method = "expanding",   config_name = "freq_np_BH" ) |>   add_alpha_test_strategy(     model_structure = "no_pooled",       # or "partial_pooled" for hierarchical pooling     p_correction_method = "BH",          # FDR control; also "holm", "BY", "bayesian", "none"     signal_significance_threshold = 0.05,     market_factor_proxy = "ibov",     enable_theme_representativeness = TRUE   )  ss_results <- run_ss_backtest(   config                  = ss_config,   signals_m_df            = gold_signals_m_df,   port_backtest_cohort    = cohort,        # backtested returns of each candidate signal   benchmark_returns_m_xts = ibov_m_xts,   signal_themes_m_df      = signal_themes_m_df )                                          # -> ss_backtest_results

previous_headings: A tour of the four workflows
what: 3. Signal blending: from heuristics to machine learning
title: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals
text: Combine selected signals one score per stock. Treat signals assets (equal-weight, risk parity, HRP, MVO), predict returns ML (glmnet, ranger, xgboost, keras), behind one interface, one tuning API (grid / random / Bayesian optimization), one walk-forward scheme. Individual blenders can stacked meta-ensemble create_sb_metabacktest_config(), predictions interpretable end end via global-surrogate feature importance explain_prediction().
code: glmnet_config <- create_sb_backtest_config(   sb_algorithm = "glmnet", target_fwd_name = "fwd_act_cum_ret_3m",   training_sample_size = 120, rebalancing_months = 1:12,   config_name = "glmnet_bayes" ) |>   add_tuning_strategy(tuning_method = "bayesian_opt",                       validation_sample_size = 36, chosen_eval_metric = "rmse",                       n_iter = 25, init_points = 8, k_iter = 8, acq = "ucb") |>   add_hyperparameter(hyperparameter = c("alpha", "lambda.min.ratio"),                      bounds = list(c(0, 1), c(1e-4, 0.5)))  sb_results <- run_sb_backtest(   features_m_df       = gold_m_df,   target_m_df         = target_m_df,   config              = glmnet_config,   ss_backtest_results = ss_results         # only the point-in-time-selected signals are used )                                          # -> sb_backtest_results

title: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals
text: --sample blended score feeds straight back portfolio engine: now full machinery concentration, turnover liquidity constraints, signal shrinkage, transaction costs, auditable trade log, portfolio methods equal-weight novel micro-macro allocation framework (MMAF).
code: final_bt <- run_port_backtest(   signals_m_df        = gold_m_df,   fwd_return_m_df     = fwd_1m_returns_m_df,   liquidity_m_df      = liquidity_m_df,   volatility_m_df     = volatility_m_df,   config              = deploy_config,   sb_backtest_results = sb_results         # per-stock `pred` drives the final weights )

title: Add Alpha Test Strategy to ss_backtest_config — add_alpha_test_strategy
code: add_alpha_test_strategy(object, alpha_test_strategy, ...)  # S4 method for class 'ss_backtest_config,alpha_test_strategy' add_alpha_test_strategy(object, alpha_test_strategy)  # S4 method for class 'ss_backtest_config,missing' add_alpha_test_strategy(   object,   signal_significance_threshold = 0.05,   p_correction_method = "none",   market_factor_proxy,   model_structure = "partial_pooled",   theme_level_intercept = NULL,   theme_level_slope = NULL,   enable_theme_representativeness = TRUE,   bayesian_model_parameters = NULL,   lmer_control = NULL )

title: Add Alpha Test Strategy to ss_backtest_config — add_alpha_test_strategy
text: object ss_backtest_config object. alpha_test_strategy alpha_test_strategy object added. ... Additional arguments passed method. signal_significance_threshold decimal indicating hypothesis testing negative-alpha null-hypothesis rejection criteria. one wants select chosen_signals, provide 1. case, signal selected demands significant CAPM alpha. p_correction_method method p-value correction. Possible options : "none": correction. "bayesian": bayesian set, hierarchical mixed-effects bayesian linear model fitted data, using brms package, interface Stan probabilistic programming language. user can also choose one following frequentist methods, control Family-Wise Error Rate (FWER) False Discovery Rate (FDR). FDR less stringent FWER. FWER, possible options : "bonferroni": Bonferroni correction, dominated Holm's method. "holm": Holm's (1979) method. "hochberg": Hochberg's (1988) method, valid hypothesis tests independent non-negatively associated. Less powerful Hommel's (1988) method, faster compute. "hommel": Hommel's (1988) method, also valid hypothesis tests independent non-negatively associated, powerful Hochberg (1988). FDR, possible options : "BH" "fdr": Benjamini-Hochberg (1995) procedure. "": Benjamini-Yekutieli (2001) procedure. market_factor_proxy character string indicating market factor proxy used CAPM model. correspond one columns benchmark_returns_df. model_structure character describing model structure. theme_level_intercept character indicating specification theme level intercept theme_level_slope character indicating specification theme level slope enable_theme_representativeness logical indicating whether, given theme signal_themes_m_df eligible signal, signal highest alpha t-stat elected. bayesian_model_parameters object class bayesian_model_parameters, containing parameters needed build hierarhicical bayesian model specify priors. lmer_control list containing control parameters lmer function.

what: Add Prior to Bayesian Alpha Test Strategy — add_brms_prior
title: Add Prior to Bayesian Alpha Test Strategy — add_brms_prior
text: Adds prior bayesian_alpha_test_strategy object based specified effect, type, distribution, parameters.

title: Add Prior to Bayesian Alpha Test Strategy — add_brms_prior
code: add_brms_prior(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'bayesian_alpha_test_strategy' add_brms_prior(   object,   effect,   type,   theme = NULL,   distribution_choice,   pars,   level = "signals" )  # S4 method for class 'ss_backtest_config' add_brms_prior(   object,   effect,   type,   theme = NULL,   distribution_choice,   pars,   level = "signals" )

title: Add Prior to Bayesian Alpha Test Strategy — add_brms_prior
text: object object class bayesian_alpha_test_strategy. ... Additional arguments. effect character string specifying effect prior. Must one "fixed" "random". type character string specifying type prior. Options: "intercept", "slope", "sigma", "cor". theme character vector specifying themes (e.g., "value", "momentum"). Applicable fixed effects. distribution_choice character vector specifying distribution prior (e.g., "normal", "student_t"). pars list named numeric vectors specifying parameters chosen distribution. level character string specifying hierarchical level random effects. Options: "signals" (default), "tickers", "theme".

title: Add Prior to Bayesian Alpha Test Strategy — add_brms_prior
text: updated bayesian_alpha_test_strategy object added prior.

title: Add Prior to Bayesian Alpha Test Strategy — add_brms_prior
text: effect = "fixed", can specify global intercepts slopes, theme-specific priors using theme argument. effect = "random", can define hierarchical priors level specifying grouping structure. Supported distribution_choice options include: "normal": Requires pars mean sd. "student_t": Requires pars df, mean, sd. "lkj": Requires pars eta (type = "cor").

title: Add covariance estimation method to a backtest configuration — add_cov_est_method
code: add_cov_est_method(object, cov_est_method, ...)  # S4 method for class 'sb_backtest_config,cov_est_method' add_cov_est_method(object, cov_est_method, ...)  # S4 method for class 'sb_backtest_config,missing' add_cov_est_method(   object,   cov_est_method,   cov_estimation_method = "sample",   cov_matrix_sample_size = 36,   active_returns = TRUE,   cov_matrix_benchmark = NULL,   ... )  # S4 method for class 'port_backtest_config,cov_est_method' add_cov_est_method(object, cov_est_method, ...)  # S4 method for class 'port_backtest_config,missing' add_cov_est_method(   object,   cov_est_method,   cov_estimation_method = "sample",   cov_matrix_sample_size = 252,   active_returns = TRUE,   cov_matrix_benchmark = NULL,   ... )

title: Add covariance estimation method to a backtest configuration — add_cov_est_method
text: object object class port_backtest_config. cov_est_method existing object class cov_est_method. ... Additional arguments. cov_estimation_method character string representing covariance estimation method. Must one "sample", "ewma", "cc", "pca1", "pca2", "shrink_id" "shrink_cc". cov_matrix_sample_size Number periods subset return sample estimating covariance matrix. high number provides active_returns logical. TRUE, covariance matrix estimated using active returns. FALSE, raw returns used. cov_matrix_benchmark character string representing benchmark covariance matrix estimation. used cov_estimation_method "shrink_id" "shrink_cc".

title: Allocate Portfolio and Compute Transaction Costs — allocate_port
code: allocate_port(   port_weights_placeholder_m_d_ref,   updated_port_weights_m_lstd_ref,   stock_universe_m_d_ref,   liquidity_m_d_ref,   volatility_m_d_ref,   main_liquidity_metric = main_liquidity_metric,   transaction_costs_parameters,   selected_benchmark_weights_m_d_ref,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Allocate Portfolio and Compute Transaction Costs — allocate_port
text: port_weights_placeholder_m_d_ref data frame containing placeholder portfolio weights. updated_port_weights_m_lstd_ref data frame containing updated portfolio weights. stock_universe_m_d_ref data frame representing stock universe. liquidity_m_d_ref data frame containing liquidity metrics. volatility_m_d_ref data frame containing volatility metrics. main_liquidity_metric name (indicator) primary liquidity metric use. default, expected defined externally explicitly passed. transaction_costs_parameters list transaction cost parameters. Expected elements : strategy_aum - Strategy Assets Management. alpha - Parameter indirect transaction cost calculation. lambda - Parameter indirect transaction cost calculation. direct_transaction_cost - Direct transaction cost value. selected_benchmark_weights_m_d_ref (Optional) data frame containing benchmark weights. provided, weights merged portfolio allocation log missing values set 0. verbose Logical indicating whether print additional messages (default TRUE).

title: Allocate Portfolio and Compute Transaction Costs — allocate_port
text: list following components: transactions_log_m_d_ref - transaction--costs log (calculate_transaction_costs()): per-ticker orders enriched direct, market-impact total costs (benchmark weights, provided). port_weights_m_d_ref - merged rescaled end--period portfolio weights. port_costs_d_ref - one-row portfolio transaction-cost summary (direct cost, market impact, total cost, turnover).

title: Allocate Portfolio and Compute Transaction Costs — allocate_port
text: function performs following steps: Initial Prep: Extracts transaction cost parameters transaction_costs_parameters. Merge Portfolio Weights: Uses merge_and_rescale_weights() merge placeholder updated weights, ensuring alignment stock universe. Calculate Transactions: Computes trade orders using calculate_trade_orders() liquidity volatility data. Compute Transaction Costs: Uses calculate_transaction_costs() determine indirect direct costs. Build Transaction Log: Extracts enriched transactions--costs data frame (transactions_log_m_d_ref) cost results. Benchmark weights, selected_benchmark_weights_m_d_ref supplied, already merged merge step (2) carried port_weights_m_d_ref transaction log.

title: Allocate Portfolio and Compute Transaction Costs — allocate_port
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Define input objects (for example, using your actual data frames)   result <- allocate_port(     port_weights_placeholder_m_d_ref = port_weights_placeholder,     updated_port_weights_m_lstd_ref = updated_port_weights,     stock_universe_m_d_ref = stock_universe,     liquidity_m_d_ref = liquidity_data,     volatility_m_d_ref = volatility_data,     main_liquidity_metric = "liquidity_metric",     transaction_costs_parameters = list(       strategy_aum = 1000000,       alpha = 0.01,       lambda = 0.02,       direct_transaction_cost = 0.005     ),     selected_benchmark_weights_m_d_ref = benchmark_weights,     verbose = TRUE   )    # Access the transaction-and-costs log:   allocation_log <- result$transactions_log_m_d_ref    # Print the allocation log using cat and paste:   cat(paste(capture.output(print(allocation_log)), collapse = "\n")) } # }

title: Compute the t-Statistic of Alpha Relative to a Benchmark — alpha_tstat_bench
text: numeric scalar equal t-statistic regression intercept. Returns NA_real_ : regression estimable (e.g., insufficient observations), benchmark zero variance, standard error alpha zero undefined, rolling window contains fewer three usable observations.

title: Compute the t-Statistic of Alpha Relative to a Benchmark — alpha_tstat_bench
text: $$ r_t = \alpha + \beta b_t + \varepsilon_t, $$ \(r_t\) denotes asset returns \(b_t\) denotes benchmark returns given rolling window. regression estimated using internal helper bench_regression_fit(), lightweight wrapper around lm.fit() designed high-volume, rolling, point--time computations. t-statistic computed : $$ t_{\alpha} = \frac{\alpha}{SE(\alpha)}, $$ standard error intercept given OLS formula: $$ SE(\alpha) = \sqrt{   \sigma^2   \left(     \frac{1}{n}     +     \frac{\bar{b}^2}{\sum (b_t - \bar{b})^2}   \right) }. $$ function designed use inside rolling computations side effects: output numeric unnamed. robust missing values return series implements strict point--time validation. OLS formulas computed manually performance reasons ensure function behaves predictably minimal rolling sample sizes.

title: Bayesian Adjustment Function — bayesian_adjustment
text: Performs Bayesian p-value adjustment setting priors, fitting Bayesian hierarchical model signals, summarizing posterior draws. Optionally provides progress updates verbose set TRUE.

title: Bayesian Adjustment Function — bayesian_adjustment
code: bayesian_adjustment(   signal_universe_m_d_ref,   selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref,   selected_market_factor_proxy_m_xts_upd_ref,   priors_m_upd_ref = NULL,   model_spec_theme_level,   user_priors = NULL,   lmer_optimization_objective = "REML",   half_t_df = 30,   lmer_optimizer = "nloptwrap",   selected_signal_themes_m_d_ref,   chains = 4,   iter = 2000,   warmup = floor(iter/2),   thin = 1,   seed = NA,   adapt_delta = 0.8,   parallel = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Bayesian Adjustment Function — bayesian_adjustment
text: signal_universe_m_d_ref Data frame. performance-enriched signal universe produced summarize_performance(), containing least id, tickers, dates, frequentist CAPM metrics (alpha, alpha_se, beta, specific_risk, alpha_t_stat, treynor_ratio, appraisal_ratio, p_value "no_pooled", theme/individual alpha beta columns "partial_pooled"). updated place posterior statistics. selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref Meta xts containing backtest returns selected signals selected_market_factor_proxy_m_xts_upd_ref Meta xts containing backtest returns selected markte factor proxy priors_m_upd_ref Data frame. (meta)data frame exogenous (--sample) return observations used derive informative priors via derive_informative_priors_from_data(), following columns: id Identifier observation. tickers Signal (ticker) identifier associated observation. dates Date observation. return Return signal date. market_factor_proxy Market factor proxy return date. theme Theme associated signal, used clustering hierarchical Bayesian model. Note: dataframe contain observations (beyond) current date. model_spec_theme_level Character string. Specifies structure hierarchical Bayesian model. Options include: "random_intercept_fixed_slope" Random intercepts signals, fixed slope across themes. "theme_specific_intercept_fixed_slope" Fixed intercepts per theme, fixed slope across themes. "theme_specific_intercept_theme_specific_slope" Fixed intercepts slopes per theme. "fixed_intercept_fixed_slope" One fixed intercept slope across observations. user_priors List. list containing user-defined priors hierarchical Bayesian model, used priors_type "user". list conform structure required brms package. lmer_optimization_objective character string indicating whether estimates chosen optimize 'REML' criterion 'likelihood'. half_t_df Numeric. degrees freedom half-t distribution applied model random effects. parameter controls tails distribution. Default 30. lmer_optimizer Character string. Specifies optimizer used lme4::lmer function. Options include: "nloptwrap" Non-linear optimization using NLopt library. "bobyqa" Bound optimization quadratic approximation. "Nelder_Mead" Simplex-based Nelder-Mead optimization. "nlminbwrap" Wrapper nlminb optimizer. Default "nloptwrap". selected_signal_themes_m_d_ref Data frame. (meta)data frame containing metadata signals following columns: id Identifier observation. tickers Signal identifiers matching signal_universe_m_d_ref selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_upd_ref. dates Dates corresponding backtest data. theme Group membership signal, defining clusters Bayesian hierarchical model. Note: dataframe contain data current date. chains Integer. number Markov chains run MCMC sampling. Default 4. iter Integer. total number iterations per chain MCMC sampling. Default 2000. warmup Integer. number warmup (burn-) iterations per chain MCMC sampling. Default floor(iter / 2). thin Integer. thinning interval MCMC sampling. Default 1. seed Integer NA. seed random number generation ensure reproducibility. Set specific integer reproducible results NA random seeding. Default NA. adapt_delta Numeric. target acceptance probability Hamiltonian Monte Carlo sampler. Higher values can lead better convergence cost slower sampling. Must 0 1. Default 0.99. parallel Logical. Indicates whether enable parallel computation using future package. Default TRUE. verbose Logical. Indicates whether print progress messages Bayesian model fitting process. TRUE, progress messages printed; otherwise, suppressed. Default TRUE.

title: Bayesian Adjustment Function — bayesian_adjustment
text: named list following components: posterior_signal_universe_m_d_ref Data frame. input signal_universe_m_d_ref updated posterior summary statistics derived Bayesian model fitting. includes metrics posterior alphas, betas, sigmas, active returns, tracking errors, information ratios (IR), appraisal ratios (AP), Treynor ratios. brm_model brmsfit object. fitted Bayesian hierarchical model containing posterior distributions, parameter estimates, diagnostics, details model fit. posterior_draws_summaries List. Summary statistics (median 89\ elected_priors brmsprior data frame, NULL uninformative priors used. priors used Bayesian model, either derived data, provided user, NULL. elected_priors_frequentist_model lme4::lmer object, NULL. fitted frequentist linear mixed-effects model used derive informative priors (set priors_m_upd_ref supplied).

title: Bayesian Adjustment Function — bayesian_adjustment
text: function performs Bayesian p-value adjustment following steps: Initial Checks: Validates input parameters, ensuring priors_type valid necessary prior data provided based specified priors_type. Set Priors: priors_m_upd_ref provided, sets priors based provided informative data using derive_informative_priors_from_data function. user_priors_list provided, utilizes user-defined priors provided. neither user_priors_list priors_m_upd_ref provided, employs default uninformative priors defined brms package. Fit Bayesian Hierarchical Model: Fits Bayesian hierarchical model theme selected_signal_themes_m_d_ref using fit_bayesian_model function. process leverages parallel processing efficiency. Progress messages displayed verbose TRUE. Extract Summarize Posteriors: Extracts posterior draws fitted models summarizes using summarize_posterior_draws function. summary includes metrics alphas, betas, sigmas, active returns, tracking errors, information ratios, appraisal ratios, Treynor ratios. Bayesian hierarchical model accounts theme-level signal-level variations, allowing nuanced adjustments based hierarchical structure data. Informative priors can derived existing data specified user, providing flexibility modeling approaches.

code: calculate_eval_metrics(   pred,   target,   huber_delta = 1,   quantile_tau = 0.5,   chosen_eval_metric = "rmse",   early_stop = NULL,   best_iteration = NULL,   return_error = FALSE )

title: Calculate Out-of-Sample Evaluation Metrics — calculate_eval_metrics
text: pred Numeric vector predicted values. target Numeric vector realized target values (length pred). huber_delta Numeric scalar, delta Pseudo-Huber error (mphe). Default 1. quantile_tau Numeric scalar (0, 1), tau Pinball error (mpe). Default 0.5. chosen_eval_metric Character, metric used build Score. One "rss", "rmse", "cp", "mae", "mphe", "mpe", "mape", "hr". Default "rmse". Note "mb" reported selectable . early_stop Numeric NULL. numeric, best_iteration column appended (xgb/nn early stopping). best_iteration Numeric NULL. Best iteration index record early_stop set. return_error Logical. TRUE, returns list metrics data frame plus raw error vector. Default FALSE.

title: Calculate Out-of-Sample Evaluation Metrics — calculate_eval_metrics
text: one-row data.frame metrics (list plus error return_error = TRUE). Columns: Score: chosen_eval_metric re-signed higher better (tuning target). rss: --sample R-squared, \(1 - \sum error^2 / \sum target^2\). cp: Mean cross-product, mean(pred * target). rmse: Root Mean Squared Error. mae: Mean Absolute Error. mphe: Mean Pseudo-Huber Error (uses huber_delta). mpe: Mean Pinball Error (uses quantile_tau). mape: Mean Absolute Percentage Error. hr: Hit Rate (share predictions whose sign matches target). mb: Mean Bias, mean(error) (reported ). best_iteration: Present early_stop numeric.

title: Calculate Out-of-Sample Evaluation Metrics — calculate_eval_metrics
text: error convention error = target - pred. every element error/target NA, prediction NA, metrics returned NA rather propagating silently. Score column single scalar tuner maximizes: chosen_eval_metric re-signed larger always better (error-type metrics rmse, mae, mphe, mpe, mape negated; rss, cp, hr kept -).

what: Compute a group-by-group (block) covariance matrix — calculate_group_covariance_matrix
title: Compute a group-by-group (block) covariance matrix — calculate_group_covariance_matrix
text: Builds \(G \times G\) matrix covariances groups (e.g., sectors) using full asset-level covariance matrix \(\Sigma\) per-group weight vectors. entry \((g_1, g_2)\) computed $$w_{g_1}^\top \, \Sigma_{g_1,g_2} \, w_{g_2},$$ \(\Sigma_{g_1,g_2}\) block \(\Sigma\) whose rows tickers group \(g_1\) columns tickers group \(g_2\).

title: Compute a group-by-group (block) covariance matrix — calculate_group_covariance_matrix
code: calculate_group_covariance_matrix(   eligible_universe_m_d_ref,   groups,   covariance_matrix,   group_col,   micro_universe_m_d_ref_list = NULL )

title: Compute a group-by-group (block) covariance matrix — calculate_group_covariance_matrix
text: eligible_universe_m_d_ref data.frame containing least columns tickers (character) grouping column indicated group_col. rows non-NA group_col values used. groups Character vector group names (e.g., sectors) compute covariance matrix. Must match values eligible_universe_m_d_ref[[group_col]]. covariance_matrix Numeric covariance matrix \(\Sigma\) row column names equal asset tickers. Must square cover tickers present eligible_universe_m_d_ref$tickers. group_col Character scalar name column eligible_universe_m_d_ref defines group membership (e.g., "sector"). micro_universe_m_d_ref_list Named list whose names elements groups. element data.frame columns: tickers (character): tickers belonging group; weights (numeric): corresponding weights group-level aggregation.

title: Compute a group-by-group (block) covariance matrix — calculate_group_covariance_matrix
text: numeric \(G \times G\) matrix dimnames set groups, entry \((g_1, g_2)\) equals \(w_{g_1}^\top \, \Sigma_{g_1,g_2} \, w_{g_2}\).

title: Compute a group-by-group (block) covariance matrix — calculate_group_covariance_matrix
text: Input requirements alignment tickers eligible_universe_m_d_ref$tickers must present row column names covariance_matrix. g groups, micro_universe_m_d_ref_list[[g]] must include tickers weights tickers belong group g according eligible_universe_m_d_ref[[group_col]]. Internally, weights aligned ticker order used extract \(\Sigma\) blocks. Normalization function renormalize group weights; results therefore reflect absolute scaling implied provided weights. need pure within-group aggregation (.e., group’s weights summing 1), normalize weights micro_universe_m_d_ref_list beforehand. Complexity computation \(O(G^2)\) block multiplications, \(G = \) length(groups), plus cost extracting block \(\Sigma\).

title: Compute a group-by-group (block) covariance matrix — calculate_group_covariance_matrix
text: function assumes eligible_universe_m_d_ref$tickers unique within group given snapshot. duplicates may occur, deduplicate aggregate beforehand.

title: Compute a group-by-group (block) covariance matrix — calculate_group_covariance_matrix
text: function raises errors : covariance_matrix numeric matrix named rows/columns. groups empty character vector. micro_universe_m_d_ref_list named list covering groups. ticker eligible_universe_m_d_ref$tickers missing covariance_matrix row/column names. group, required tickers missing corresponding micro_universe_m_d_ref_list[[g]]$tickers, weights frame lacks required tickers/weights columns.

Net Active Return: Net Return minus benchmark forward return (fwd_selected_benchmark_return supplied).

Optionally, also computes parallel set metrics provided group universe (e.g., sector sleeve portfolio), column names prefixed group_.

text: signals_m_df data frame containing signal data must coercible meta_dataframe. must least columns IDs, tickers, dates (columns 1:3) subsequent columns numeric free NAs. Dates must class Date. oos_predictions_m_df (Optional) data frame --sample predictions must coercible meta_dataframe. expected contain columns: "id", "tickers", "dates", "pred". IDs data frame must correspond IDs signals_m_df initial buffer period. chosen_score_metric_and_position (Optional) single-element list specifying chosen score metric position. name(s) must include substring "low_" chosen metric must present column name signals_m_df. rebalancing_months numeric value indicating rebalancing frequency months. Must numeric 1 12. initial_buffer_period numeric value specifying number initial periods exclude backtest. port_construction_method character string indicating portfolio construction method. Allowed values "ew", "sw", "cw", "cs", "rp", "hrp", "mvo", "mmaf", "custom_weights". eligibility_quantile_range numeric vector length 2 specifying eligibility quantile range used portfolio construction. min_eligible_assets_fallback positive integer indicating fallback minimum number eligible assets (optional). selected_benchmark character string naming benchmark used active return calculation constraints. chosen_scaler Character. Column name scaler_m_df defining scaling variable. scaler_m_df Optional meta_dataframe scaling parameters. scaler_shrinkage Numeric. Shrinkage factor scaling. use_raw_for_eligibility Logical. TRUE, uses raw scores eligibility filtering. rp_method parameter specifying risk parity method applied (applicable). exp_ret_score_tilt logical value indicating whether apply expected return score tilting risk parity mean-variance optimization (applicable). exp_ret_score_tilt_eta numeric value specifying exponent used expected return score tilting (applicable). linkage Character. Linkage method hierarchical clustering HRP. n_random_ports numeric value indicating number random portfolios generate. random_ports_method parameter specifying method generating random portfolios. opt_objective parameter defining optimization objective. opt_method parameter specifying optimization method. target_port_m_df data frame containing target portfolio weights (applicable). ridge_pen numeric value indicating ridge penalty (applicable). n_resamples numeric value specifying number resamples (applicable). exp_ret_score_jitter numeric value indicating jitter applied expected return scores (applicable). cov_eigval_jitter numeric value specifying jitter applied covariance eigenvalues (applicable). mmaf_method Character. Method micro-macro allocation framework: "bottom_up" "top_down". top_down_proxy_port_method Character. Method create top-proxy portfolio proxy. mmaf_group_col Character. Column name stock_groups_m_df defining groups MMAF. micro_port_construction_method Character. Method micro-level portfolio construction MMAF. macro_port_construction_method Character. Method macro-level portfolio construction MMAF. macro_concentration_constraint_policy list defining concentration constraints macro level. macro_n_random_ports Integer. Number random portfolios macro-level optimization. macro_random_ports_method Character. Method sample random portfolios macro level. macro_opt_objective Character. Optimization target macro-level portfolio. macro_opt_method Character. Optimization method macro-level portfolio. macro_ridge_pen Numeric. Ridge penalty macro-level optimization. macro_n_resamples Integer. Number resamples robust optimization macro level. macro_exp_ret_score_jitter Numeric. Jitter add expected return scores macro-level resampling. macro_cov_eigval_jitter Numeric. Jitter add covariance matrix eigenvalues macro-level resampling. macro_rp_method Character. Risk parity allocation method macro-level portfolio. macro_exp_ret_score_tilt Logical. TRUE, applies expected return score tilt macro-level RP. macro_exp_ret_score_tilt_eta Numeric. Exponent expected return score tilt macro-level RP. macro_linkage Character. Linkage method hierarchical clustering macro-level HRP. cov_estimation_method character string specifying covariance estimation method. Required port_construction_method "rp" "mvo". cov_matrix_sample_size numeric value indicating sample size used covariance matrix estimation. Required port_construction_method "rp" "mvo". active_returns logical value indicating whether active returns used. TRUE, daily_bench_returns_m_xts must provided. cov_matrix_benchmark character string specifying benchmark covariance matrix estimation. specified benchmark must present column daily_bench_returns_m_xts. daily_stock_returns_m_xts xts object containing daily stock returns. dates (.e., index) must class Date, consecutive, cover tickers present signals_m_df. daily_bench_returns_m_xts xts object containing daily benchmark returns. dates must match daily_stock_returns_m_xts data must contain NA values. benchmark_returns_m_xts xts object benchmark returns. Dates must class Date arranged sequential monthly dates without NAs. liquidity_constraint_policy (Optional) object representing liquidity constraint policy. validated validate_liquidity_constraint_policy requires corresponding liquidity data provided. turnover_constraint_policy (Optional) object representing turnover constraint policy. validated validate_turnover_constraint_policy requires liquidity information available. concentration_constraint_policy (Optional) object representing concentration constraint policy. validated validate_concentration_constraint_policy requires corresponding benchmark weights stock group data. liquidity_m_df data frame containing liquidity information. must coercible meta_dataframe, non-normalized numeric columns free NAs, must cover stocks signals_m_df. liquidity_floor_cutoffs data frame providing liquidity floor cutoff values. validated validate_liquidity_floor_cutoffs must include main_liquidity_metric. main_liquidity_metric character string specifying primary liquidity metric. must present liquidity_m_df include substring "mean_volfin". enable_group_representativeness logical value indicating whether enable group representativeness constraints. TRUE, stock_groups_m_df must provided. stock_groups_m_df (Optional) data frame containing stock group data, coercible meta_dataframe. stocks signals_m_df represented, group columns must type character. benchmark_weights_m_df (Optional) data frame benchmark weights. must coercible meta_dataframe, numeric columns values 0 1 (summing 1 per date), cover stocks signals_m_df. volatility_m_df data frame containing volatility data, coercible meta_dataframe. Numeric columns must NAs, include "daily_vol" column, cover stocks signals_m_df. fwd_return_m_df data frame containing forward returns, coercible meta_dataframe. must contain column named "fwd_return_1m"; data numeric (NAs allowed final dates) must match structure signals_m_df (IDs, tickers, dates). transaction_costs_parameters object (list) containing transaction cost parameters. must names "direct_transaction_cost", "strategy_aum", "alpha", "lambda", validated via validate_transaction_costs_parameters. custom_stock_weights_m_df (Optional) data frame containing custom stock universe weights coercible meta_dataframe. custom_stock_metrics_m_df (Optional) data frame containing custom stock metrics coercible meta_dataframe. user_defined_OR_rules_m_df (Optional) meta_dataframe 5 columns defining -based eligibility filters. 5th column must binary (0 1). user_defined_AND_rules_m_df (Optional) meta_dataframe 5 columns defining -based eligibility filters. 5th column must binary (0 1). lower_quantile_winsorization numeric value specifying lower winsorization quantile. upper_quantile_winsorization numeric value specifying upper winsorization quantile. verbose Logical. Whether print warnings informational messages.

Linkage method hierarchical clustering macro-level HRP. concentration_constraint_policy list/policy object defining signal-level (optionally group-level) active-weight caps. custom_signal_weights_m_df (meta) data frame user-supplied signal weights; required sb_algorithm = "custom_weights". sb_algorithm Character, choice signal blending algorithm ("ols", "glmnet", "rf", "xgb", "nn", "ew", "sw", "rp", "hrp", "mvo", "mmaf", "custom_weights"). gsm_algorithm Character, global surrogate model algorithm used interpretability ("ols" "tree"). custom_objective Character, custom objective function loss. chosen_eval_metric Character, chosen evaluation metric ("rmse", "mae", "cp", "rss", "mphe", "mpe", "hr", "mape"). huber_delta Numeric, delta parameter Huber loss ("pseudo_huber_error" custom objective). quantile_tau Numeric, tau parameter quantile loss ("quantile_error" custom objective). hyper_grid_domain_list List, domain list hyperparameters tuning. tuning_method Character, method hyperparameter tuning ("random_search", "grid_search", "bayesian_opt"). n_iter number iterations tuning. k_iter number iterations k-fold cross-validation. acq Character, acquisition function Bayesian optimization ("ucb", "ei", "poi"). init_points Numeric, number initial points Bayesian optimization. early_stop Numeric, number epochs early stopping ("xgb" "nn" algorithms). keras_architecture_parameters List, containing units (numeric), n_layers (numeric 1 5), activation_function nn_optimizer ("Adam" "RMSProp") parallel Logical, whether use parallel computation. verbose Logical, whether print verbose output. .test_seed Optional numeric seed used make validation reproducible tests.

title: Perform Validation Checks on Inputs for Signal Selection (SS) Workflow — check_inputs_ss_backtest
code: check_inputs_ss_backtest(   initial_sample_size,   rebalancing_months,   split_method,   signals_m_df,   chosen_signals_and_positions,   forced_signals,   custom_signal_universe_metrics_m_df,   backtest_returns_m_xts,   benchmark_returns_m_xts,   market_factor_proxy,   active_returns,   p_correction_method,   signal_significance_threshold,   enable_theme_representativeness,   model_structure,   theme_level_intercept,   theme_level_slope,   lmer_control,   priors_m_df,   user_priors,   brms_control,   prior_derivation_control,   signal_themes_m_df,   lower_quantile_winsorization,   upper_quantile_winsorization )

title: Perform Validation Checks on Inputs for Signal Selection (SS) Workflow — check_inputs_ss_backtest
text: initial_sample_size Numeric. minimum number observations required first backtest rebalancing can executed. rebalancing_months Numeric. Vector months (1–12) signal selection implemented. split_method Character. Method used split sample data backtest (e.g., expanding rolling). signals_m_df (meta) data frame columns id, tickers, dates, additional numeric columns representing signals. contain NAs columns prefixed "low_". chosen_signals_and_positions named character vector specifying selected signals positions. Example: c(book_to_market = "long", vol_12m = "short"). forced_signals named character vector signals forcibly included. Values must "force". custom_signal_universe_metrics_m_df meta-data frame custom metrics (optional). Must contain rows equal number signals times number dates, include columns default performance metric output. backtest_returns_m_xts xts object signal-specific backtested returns. Column names must correspond signals used. benchmark_returns_m_xts xts object containing benchmark returns, aligned dates backtest_returns_m_xts. market_factor_proxy Character string. Name column benchmark_returns_m_xts used market factor. active_returns Logical. TRUE, returns adjusted subtracting benchmark returns performance evaluation. p_correction_method Character. Specifies method p-value correction. Options include: "none" "bayesian" Frequentist FWER: "bonferroni", "holm", "hochberg", "hommel" Frequentist FDR: "BH", "fdr", "" signal_significance_threshold Numeric. Threshold selecting signals based CAPM alpha significance (e.g., 0.05). Use 1 bypass selection threshold. enable_theme_representativeness Logical. TRUE, ensures least one signal per theme selected, based highest t-statistic. model_structure Character. Either "partial_pooled" "no_pooled" control hierarchical modeling strategy. theme_level_intercept Character. Specifies intercepts modeled theme level (used model_structure == "partial_pooled"). theme_level_slope Character. Specifies slopes modeled theme level (used model_structure == "partial_pooled"). lmer_control list options passed lme4::lmer. Accepted elements: lmer_optimizer: Optimization method. One "nloptwrap", "bobyqa", "Nelder_Mead", "nlminbwrap". lmer_optimization_objective: Either "REML" "likelihood". hierarchical_p_value_method: P-value method. One "Satterthwaite", "Kenward-Roger", "lme4". priors_m_df (meta) data frame columns id, tickers, dates, return, market_factor_proxy, theme. Used derive informative priors. user_priors data.frame brmsprior objects. Must match expected structure according model_spec_theme_level. brms_control list parameters passed brms::brm: chains: Integer, number MCMC chains (default 4). iter: Integer, iterations per chain (default 2000). warmup: Integer, warm-iterations (default floor(iter / 2)). thin: Integer, thinning interval (default 1). seed: Integer NA, reproducibility (default NA). adapt_delta: Numeric 0 1 (default 0.99). prior_derivation_control list controls frequentist prior derivation: half_t_df: Degrees freedom half-t priors scale parameters. signal_themes_m_df (meta) data frame columns id, tickers, dates, theme. Defines signal group structure hierarchical modeling. lower_quantile_winsorization Numeric 0 1. Lower winsorization threshold signal metrics. upper_quantile_winsorization Numeric 0 1. Upper winsorization threshold signal metrics.

title: Classify the universe based on signals and other custom and user-defined rules. — classify_investment_universe
text: universe_m_d_ref data frame stocks signals. eligibility_quantile_range numeric vector length 2 indicating range quantiles used filtering stocks. min_eligible_assets_fallback numeric value indicating minimum number eligible assets selected. signal_significance_threshold numeric value indicating threshold signal significance. liquidity_floor_cutoffs Mandatory turnover_constraint_policy /liquidity_constraint_policy provided. list named vectors containing cutoff values classify stocks according liquidity. element must named according 5 following liquidity classifications: ("micro_caps", "small_caps", "mid_caps", "large_caps" "mega_caps) vector must provide named numeric values indicate minimum acceptable values (adjusted inflation) stocks classification. Classification ascending order (least liquid liquid) metrics. set decimals, values interpreted quantiles classification set accordingly. Stocks liquidity lower micro_caps receive nano_caps classification. liquidity_m_d_ref data frame  containing columns id, tickers, dates, one market liquidity measures (e.g., inflation-adjusted mean financial volume). tickers current universe must unique correspondence data frame. liquidity_constraint_policy Optional. named list containing objects used apply liquidity constraints. Possible elements list : liquidity_floor_rule: character indicating liquidity classification (e.g., micro_caps, small_caps) used filter stocks. Stocks less liquidity specified liquidity_floor_rule considered ineligible. case generate_box_constraints function, liquidity_constraint_policy can also contain: liquidity_cap_rule lists: One many lists used create upper bounds weights based liquidity classification. list must contain: liquidity_classification: character indicating classification cap. liquidity_cap: numeric value indicating cap (upper bound) stocks liquidity classification. Many liquidity caps might created, case, liquidity_cap_rule must identified number (e.g., liquidity_cap_rule_1, liquidity_cap_rule_2, ). updated_port_weights_m_lstd_ref data frame containing columns id, tickers, dates, weights old portfolio (pre-rebalancing). tickers current stock universe must unique correspondence data frame. turnover_constraint_policy named list containing objects used build buffer zones apply turnover constraints. element constitute buffer_zone, list three elements: liquidity_classification element: liquidity classification (e.g., "micro_caps", "small_caps") buffer zone. top_quantile_buffer: numeric value indicating buffer value relaxes pre_eligible_assets stocks specified liquidity classification. turnover_cap: numeric value specifying turnover cap. Stocks less liquid specified buffer zone signal higher respective buffer quantile considered eligible, even meet liquidity_floor_rule. selected_benchmark character vector describing strategy benchmark. benchmark_weights_m_d_ref data frame containing columns id, tickers, dates, current benchmark weights columns. tickers current universe must unique correspondence data frame. groups_m_d_ref data frame containing columns id, tickers, dates, group classification columns following given classification method. tickers current universe must unique correspondence data frame. concentration_constraint_policy named list containing four elements: benchmark: character vector describing benchmark used apply constraint. Must correspondence benchmark_weights_m_d_ref max_abs_active_individual_weight: maximum absolute individual active weights. max_abs_active_group_weight: maximum absolute group active weight used creating group constraints generate_group_constraints. given group eligible asset, one greatest signal automatically promoted. Note , context generate_group_constraints, benchmark_weights_m_d_ref data frame must also supplied. enable_group_representativeness Logical. TRUE, ensures least one asset group classification included eligible universe. is_mmaf TRUE, indicates concentration_constraint_policy refers macro level thus forces group eligibility. target_port_m_d_ref Optional. data frame containing columns id, tickers, dates, target portfolio weights. ridge_pen Optional. numeric value indicating ridge penalty used shrinking MVO weights towards target portfolio. user_defined_AND_rules_m_d_ref Optional. named list named data frames containing column tickers, columns metrics passed final data frame, column describes filter name list element. example, apply filter stocks begin , user_defined_AND_rules_m_d_ref can contain data frame element named "starts_with_A_rule". data frame can contain metric column (e.g., name stock), descriptive filter column must named "starts_with_A_rule". case, "starts_with_A_rule" column integer, values must either 1L (stock passes rule) 0L (stock fails pass rule). rule appended filter regular promotion rule, accumulating rules. user_defined_OR_rules_m_d_ref Optional. named list named data frames containing column tickers, columns metrics passed final data frame, column describes filter name list element. tickers current stock universe must unique correspondence data frame. asset_object character indicating whether analysis applied "stocks" "signal_portfolios" use_raw_for_eligibility Logical. TRUE, uses raw scores eligibility filtering. verbose logical indicating whether print messages function execution.

title: Classify the universe based on signals and other custom and user-defined rules. — classify_investment_universe
text: function provides additional custom rules also accepts user-defined rules.

title: Classify the universe based on signals and other custom and user-defined rules. — classify_investment_universe
text: promoted eligible, assets must meet one following criteria: Regular Eligibility Top Assets Rule: Asset must top quantile specified top_quantile. ignore behavior, set top_quantile 0. Liquidity Floor Rule (exclusive stocks): must meet minimum liquidity requirements defined liquidity floor rule. Active Weights Constraint Policy Eligibility: Maximum Absolute Individual Active Weight Rule (exlusive stocks): Benchmark weight must exceed maximum absolute individual active weight threshold. Turnover Policy Eligibility: (exclusive stocks) Stock must one buffer zones. happen: Stock must top quantile buffer (signal >= top_quantile_buffer). Stock must pre-rebalancing portfolio. Stock must meet liquidity classification buffer zone. user_defined_OR_rules Eligibility (currently implemented stocks) Group Representativeness Eligibility: stocks signal portfolios one groups specified concentration_constraint_policy, representative included according best quantile. user_defined_AND_rules

title: Classify the universe based on signals and other custom and user-defined rules. — classify_investment_universe
text: Active Weights Constraint Policy Eligibility dominant; assets meeting rule always eligible. Turnover Policy Eligibility takes precedence Liquidity Floor Rule; thus, stock buffer zone included even liquidity floor rule suggests otherwise. Assets meet user_defined_OR_rules always promoted. Assets fail meet user_defined_AND_rules always excluded.

title: Classify the universe based on signals and other custom and user-defined rules. — classify_investment_universe
text: function classifies stock universes (asset_object = "stocks") signal-portfolio universes (asset_object = "signals"). stocks, pre-eligibility Top Assets quantile rule exp_ret_score. signals, pre-eligibility instead driven statistical significance CAPM alpha: pd_alpha column present, Bayesian rule 1 - pd_alpha <= signal_significance_threshold used; otherwise frequentist rule adjusted_p_value <= signal_significance_threshold & alpha > 0 applied (using alpha column -pooled case, individual_alpha partial-pooled case). shared logic classify_investment_universe() reused inside define_signal_eligibility().

what: Build aggregated group-level universe objects (and optional group covariance) — compute_agg_macro_objects
title: Build aggregated group-level universe objects (and optional group covariance) — compute_agg_macro_objects
text: Aggregates stock-level information group-level universe (e.g., sectors) single date snapshot, optionally computing: weighted group summaries (expected return score liquidity metrics), group--group covariance matrix using asset-level covariance. Internally, within group function builds per-group weight vector (eligible_universe_m_d_ref). group's weights sum zero, falls back equal-weights group warns . final outputs : group_universe_m_d_ref: one row per group aggregated metrics; group_covariance_matrix: optional \(G \times G\) covariance groups; group_liquidity_m_d_ref: optional group-level liquidity frame aligned liquidity_m_d_ref's columns; micro_universe_m_d_ref_list: per-group weights actually used.

title: Build aggregated group-level universe objects (and optional group covariance) — compute_agg_macro_objects
code: compute_agg_macro_objects(   universe_m_d_ref,   covariance_matrix = NULL,   group_col,   micro_universe_m_d_ref_list = NULL,   liquidity_m_d_ref = NULL )

title: Build aggregated group-level universe objects (and optional group covariance) — compute_agg_macro_objects
text: universe_m_d_ref data.frame. Must contain least: tickers (character): asset identifiers; dates (Date): snapshot date; is_eligible (integer/logical): must 1 0; exp_ret_score (numeric): stock-level expected return score (used aggregation); weights (numeric): portfolio weights snapshot (used aggregate within group); {group_col} (character): grouping column name provided via group_col. covariance_matrix matrix NULL. Optional asset-level covariance \(\Sigma\) whose row/column names exactly eligible_universe_m_d_ref$tickers. provided, group covariance matrix computed via \(\Sigma_G[g_1,g_2] = w_{g_1}^\top \Sigma_{g_1,g_2} w_{g_2}\) using per-group weights micro_universe_m_d_ref_list. group_col character(1). Column name eligible_universe_m_d_ref defines group membership (e.g., "Sector"). micro_universe_m_d_ref_list NULL named list. Optional override within-group weights. Names must cover groups present eligible_universe_m_d_ref[[group_col]]. element data.frame columns: tickers (character); weights (numeric) — expected within-group weights (function normalize sum nonzero; sum zero, falls back EW warning). NULL, function constructs list eligible_universe_m_d_ref. liquidity_m_d_ref NULL data.frame. Optional stock-level liquidity metrics aligned id/tickers/dates. columns first three (id, tickers, dates) aggregated weighted mean using group's within-group weights. NULL, liquidity aggregation produced.

title: Build aggregated group-level universe objects (and optional group covariance) — compute_agg_macro_objects
text: list components: group_universe_m_d_ref (data.frame): one row per group containing: id (character): "group-current_date"; tickers (character): group identifier (group name); dates (Date): snapshot date; {liquidity columns} (numeric, optional): weighted means liquidity metric provided liquidity_m_d_ref (); {*_bench_weights}/{target_weights} (numeric, optional): group-level sums matching columns found eligible_universe_m_d_ref; exp_ret_score (numeric): weighted mean stock exp_ret_score using within-group weights; is_eligible (integer): set 1 groups retained. group_covariance_matrix (matrix NULL): group--group covariance matrix (dimnames = groups), NULL covariance_matrix NULL. group_liquidity_m_d_ref (data.frame NULL): group-level liquidity frame containing columns liquidity_m_d_ref (provided), else NULL. micro_universe_m_d_ref_list (named list): within-group weights used aggregation group covariance computation.

title: Build aggregated group-level universe objects (and optional group covariance) — compute_agg_macro_objects
text: Groups inferred eligible_universe_m_d_ref[[group_col]] provided snapshot. function enforces: eligible_universe_m_d_ref contains eligible rows (is_eligible == 1); covariance_matrix provided, row/column names exactly eligible_universe_m_d_ref$tickers; micro_universe_m_d_ref_list NULL, per-group weights built eligible_universe_m_d_ref$weights normalized within group; group's sum weights zero, falls back equal-weights warning. Aggregation rules exp_ret_score: stats::weighted.mean() within group using group's weights. Liquidity columns: column liquidity_m_d_ref (except first three identifier columns) aggregated stats::weighted.mean() using within-group weights. Group {*_bench_weights}/target_weights: computed dplyr::summarise() sums across constituent stocks group. Group covariance (optional) covariance_matrix provided, function calls calculate_group_covariance_matrix() per-group weights list returns \(\Sigma_G\). coherent ex-ante risk decomposition group weights reflect portfolio's within-group exposures.

title: Build aggregated group-level universe objects (and optional group covariance) — compute_agg_macro_objects
text: Errors: missing group_col; non-matching ticker sets eligible_universe_m_d_ref$tickers covariance_matrix (provided); invalid micro_universe_m_d_ref_list structure; presence ineligible rows. Warnings: group's weights sum zero, function falls back equal-weights group (message: "Weights group 'g' sum zero. Fallback equal weights.").

title: Compute Rolling or Seasonal Calculations for a Given Metric in meta_dataframe or meta_xts — compute_window
text: data meta_dataframe meta_xts object. period numeric value indicating time window: FUN character specifying function apply. Supported options depend input class: meta_dataframe: "sum", "mean", "geom_mean_ret", "median", "sd", "skew", "sur", "cagr", "signal_to_noise", "res_mom", "idio_vol", "count_if", "max", "min", "lag". meta_xts: "mean", "median", "geom_mean_ret", "max", "min", "sd", "skew", "sur", "cagr", "signal_to_noise", "alpha", "alpha_tstat", "beta", "correlation", "res_mom", "idio_vol". benchmark-based FUNs ("res_mom", "idio_vol", "alpha", "alpha_tstat", "beta", "correlation") require benchmark_returns_m_xts selected_bench. ... Additional arguments passed function. window character specifying window type: either "rolling" (default) "seasonal". signal (meta_dataframe) character specifying column name rolling function computed. benchmark_returns_m_xts meta_xts object. Required FUN "res_mom" "idio_vol". selected_bench character specifying column name benchmark_returns_m_xts@data use. Required FUN "res_mom" "idio_vol". na.rm logical indicating whether remove NA values (default: TRUE). only_unique logical indicating whether compute metric using unique values (default: FALSE). feature_name character specifying name computed feature. NULL (default), feature name set "<metric>_<window>_<period>_<FUN>". min_non_na numeric value specifying minimum number non-NA values required compute rolling statistic. Default 0. Exception: FUN = "cagr", default period + 1 ensure sufficient periods. count_condition_fun function takes numeric vector returns logical vector. function return TRUE elements counted. used FUN = "count_if". offset_months numeric value specifying number months offset ahead backwards seasonal window type. col_name (meta_xts) character specifying column name. specific_dates Date vector specifying specific dates consider rolling calculation. mult_last_n numeric value indicating number recent observations multiply factor computing certain metrics (default: 0). mult_by numeric value indicating multiplication factor last n observations computing certain metrics (default: -1 metrics, 0 "geom_mean_ret"). top_n numeric value indicating number top elements consider computing "max" function (default: 1).

title: Compute Rolling or Seasonal Calculations for a Given Metric in meta_dataframe or meta_xts — compute_window
text: modified meta_dataframe meta_xts object additional column named "<metric>_<window>_<period>_<FUN>", storing computed values.

title: Compute Rolling or Seasonal Calculations for a Given Metric in meta_dataframe or meta_xts — compute_window
text: window can either: Rolling: Includes dates within range [current_date - period months, current_date]. Fwd Seasonal: Includes observations ticker whose months match consecutive months immediately following current observation's month. function filters observations within specified time window applies selected FUN. matching observations found, result NA. Available functions: sum: sum(x, na.rm = na.rm) (meta_dataframe ). mean: mean(x, na.rm = na.rm). geom_mean_ret: Geometric mean return window (percentage-point scale). median: stats::median(x, na.rm = na.rm). sd: stats::sd(x, na.rm = na.rm). skew: Bias-adjusted skewness values window. sur: (final_value - mean(x, na.rm = na.rm)) / stats::sd(x, na.rm = na.rm), final_value recent value. cagr: Compound growth rate first last value window. signal_to_noise: mean(x) / sd(x) (mean--standard-deviation ratio). res_mom: Rolling regression metric benchmark; returns standardized sum residuals (residual momentum). idio_vol: Rolling regression benchmark; returns sqrt(sd(metric)^2 - beta^2 * sd(benchmark)^2). alpha, alpha_tstat, beta, correlation: Rolling CAPM-style statistics benchmark (meta_xts ). count_if: Counts number elements satisfy count_condition_fun (meta_dataframe ). max: max(x, na.rm = na.rm) meta_dataframe; sum top top_n values meta_xts. min: min(x, na.rm = na.rm). lag: Retrieves observation happened period months ago (meta_dataframe ).

Must one 'sample', 'ewma', 'cc', 'pca1', 'pca2', 'shrink_id' 'shrink_cc'. cov_matrix_sample_size Number periods subset return sample estimating covariance matrix. high number provide higher degrees freedom, old returns might reflect current risk due parameter shift. low number tend expose estimation dimensionality curse. active_returns logical. title: Create an alpha_test_strategy object — create_alpha_test_strategy
text: Constructor objects class alpha_test_strategy subclasses (frequentist_alpha_test_strategy, bayesian_alpha_test_strategy). function validates model configuration constructs appropriate strategy object based hypothesis testing methodology hierarchical model structure.

title: Create an alpha_test_strategy object — create_alpha_test_strategy
code: create_alpha_test_strategy(   model_structure = "no_pooled",   theme_level_intercept = NULL,   theme_level_slope = NULL,   signal_significance_threshold = 0.05,   p_correction_method = "none",   market_factor_proxy,   bayesian_model_parameters = NULL,   enable_theme_representativeness = TRUE,   lmer_control = NULL )

title: Create an alpha_test_strategy object — create_alpha_test_strategy
text: model_structure Character string specifying hierarchical model structure. Must one : "partial_pooled": Uses theme-level effects partial pooling. "no_pooled": pooling; estimates signal-level parameters independently. theme_level_intercept Character string indicating intercepts modeled theme level (used model_structure = "partial_pooled"). Valid options : "fixed": common intercept across themes. "random": Intercepts modeled random effects. "theme_specific": theme fixed intercept. Must NULL model_structure = "no_pooled". theme_level_slope Character string indicating slopes modeled theme level (used model_structure = "partial_pooled"). Valid options : "fixed": common slope across themes. "theme_specific": theme slope. Must NULL model_structure = "no_pooled". signal_significance_threshold Numeric. Significance level alpha tests (e.g., 0.05). Determines rejection region null hypothesis zero alpha. Must 0 1. p_correction_method Character string specifying p-value adjustment method used multiple hypothesis testing correction. Options include: "none": correction. "bonferroni", "holm", "hochberg", "hommel": Classical FWER control methods. "BH", "fdr", "": FDR control methods. "bayesian": Use Bayesian hypothesis testing instead p-values. market_factor_proxy Character string specifying identifier market factor proxy. variable typically used market return CAPM-style alpha test model. bayesian_model_parameters optional object class bayesian_model_parameters. p_correction_method = "bayesian", must either provided explicitly initialized default (uninformative) prior settings. Ignored frequentist strategies. enable_theme_representativeness Logical. TRUE, enables extra diagnostics modeling logic account representativeness theme level. Useful themes considered key grouping variables inference. lmer_control Optional. list control parameters passed lme4::lmer() (frequentist) brms::brm() (Bayesian) customizing model fitting behavior. Can include convergence tolerances, optimizers, verbosity flags.

title: Create an alpha_test_strategy object — create_alpha_test_strategy
text: object class: frequentist_alpha_test_strategy, frequentist method selected; bayesian_alpha_test_strategy, Bayesian inference selected. returned object can passed downstream workflows evaluate classify alpha signals.

title: Create a meta_dataframe — create_meta_dataframe
text: data list element matrix, data.frame tibble representing feature. elements must identical dimensions (rows = entities, columns = time points). meta_dataframe_name Character. Name resulting object (default: "not_identified"). ... Additional arguments (ignored many branches). workflow Optional list. Workflow entries stored workflow slot. ss_backtest_workflow Optional list. Required type = "signal_universe". sb_backtest_workflow Optional list. Required type = "oos_sb_outputs". port_backtest_workflow Optional list. Required type = "stock_universe". type Character. Determines subclass instantiated (see generic type values). "generic" method warns missing monthly dates still constructs object; specialized type values enforce additional required workflow args. tickers Character vector. Row identifiers elements data; must unique length equal nrow element. dates Date vector. Column identifiers elements data; must Date, unique, day--month, consecutive month (monthly panel). features_names Character vector. Names assigned element data; length must equal length(data). data_format Character. Currently "wide" supported; method errors otherwise. tickers_on Character. Currently "rows" supported; method errors otherwise.

title: Create a meta_dataframe — create_meta_dataframe
text: S4 object class meta_dataframe appropriate subclass. Populated slots: data, signals, unique_dates, unique_tickers, n_obs, current_date, meta_dataframe_name, optional workflow/backtest slots. object class meta_dataframe one subclasses metadata: unique_dates, unique_tickers, n_obs, current_date, signals, meta_dataframe_name. raw_features_m_df (subclass meta_dataframe) containing long-format data.frame id, tickers, dates one column per feature (named features_names), plus metadata slots.

title: Create a meta_dataframe — create_meta_dataframe
text: Create meta_dataframe Object Prefer high-level constructor calling new(...) directly performs structural validation populates metadata consistently. generic dispatches — see method-specific documentation exact validation rules error conditions (number tests assert validations). — Validation performed method (tests assert behaviours) Calls is_coercible_to_meta_dataframe() checks: required columns, dates class, id format, ordering, uniqueness NA presence. type == "generic" warning emitted missing months sequence (monthly cadence). specialized types method requires corresponding workflow argument: ss_backtest_workflow signal_universe, sb_backtest_workflow oos_sb_outputs, port_backtest_workflow stock_universe. — Validation performed method (tests assert behaviours) data must list; element must matrix, data.frame, tibble. elements must identical numbers rows columns; otherwise error thrown (tests expect messages "elements list must number rows/columns."). features_names length must equal length(data). element may consist entirely NA; otherwise error ("One datasets contain NA values."). tickers must unique character vector whose length equals nrow elements; errors non-character duplicated tickers. dates must class Date, unique, share day--month, consecutive month; failures raise informative errors used tests. Elements must already contain columns named tickers dates; elements must contain values matching provided tickers dates (conditions raise errors asserted tests). data_format = "wide" tickers_on = "rows" accepted.

title: Create a meta_dataframe — create_meta_dataframe
text: create_meta_dataframe(data.frame): Create meta_dataframe (subclass) long-format data.frame. create_meta_dataframe(list): Create meta_dataframe structured list wide feature matrices/tibbles.

title: Create a meta_dataframe — create_meta_dataframe
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # See the data.frame and list method examples below. } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- data.frame(   id = c("A-2026-01-31","B-2026-01-31"),   tickers = c("A","B"),   dates = as.Date(c("2026-01-31","2026-01-31")),   momentum = c(0.1, -0.2),   value = c(0.05, 0.03),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) mdf <- create_meta_dataframe(df, meta_dataframe_name = "features_example", type = "generic") # For signal_universe: su <- create_meta_dataframe(df, meta_dataframe_name = "signal_universe_example",                             type = "signal_universe",                             ss_backtest_workflow = list(params = "example")) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ tickers <- c("Stock A","Stock B") dates <- as.Date(c("2001-03-15","2001-04-15")) feat1 <- matrix(c(0,1,2,3), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) feat2 <- matrix(c(4,5,6,7), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) feat3 <- matrix(c(8,9,10,11), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE) features_list <- list(Alpha = feat1, Beta = feat2, Gamma = feat3)  raw_mdf <- create_meta_dataframe(features_list,                                  tickers = tickers,                                  dates = dates,                                  features_names = c("Alpha","Beta","Gamma"),                                  meta_dataframe_name = "raw_features_example") } # }

title: Create a meta_xts Object (returns_meta_xts or metrics_meta_xts) — create_meta_xts
text: data xts object, data.frame, meta_dataframe containing time series data. type Character. Either "returns" (built returns_meta_xts, holes allowed) "metrics" (built metrics_meta_xts, holes allowed). Defaults "returns" (first value c("returns", "metrics"), resolved via match.arg()). asset_type Character. Type asset returns_meta_xts objects (e.g. "stock", "ports"). Defaults "not_identified", emits message() type = "returns". meta_xts_name Character. label resulting object(s). Defaults "not_identified". metric_name Character. Name metric/return series. NULL, defaults "returns"/"metrics" xts/wide-data.frame input, feature column name(s) long-data.frame input. vector supplied long input must match number feature columns length. workflow object recording processing history. Defaults NULL. meta_dataframe input, source object's workflow carried coercion entry appended. source character vector indicating data origin column. NULL, defaults "not_identified" repeated column. ... Additional arguments passed underlying method. data_format Character. data.frame input : "wide" (columns already assets/metrics) "long" (requires tickers dates columns; non-id column becomes separate result). Defaults "wide". dates optional vector dates, sorted ascending. wide input supplies time index directly; long input overrides pivoted dates column. Unsorted input error rather silently re-sorted, data caller believes already aligned never reordered without knowledge.
