what: Create a Micro–Macro Allocation Framework (MMAF) portfolio — create_mmaf_portfolio
title: Create a Micro–Macro Allocation Framework (MMAF) portfolio — create_mmaf_portfolio
text: Implements two-layer portfolio construction framework behavior controlled mmaf_method: "top_down": Build proxy intra-sector portfolios first (sector budgets), aggregate sector-level metrics, construct sector (macro) weights, re-optimize inside sector sector budget (constraints scaled sector budget), finally reconcile final stock weights (sector weight × intra-sector weight). "bottom_up": Build single micro-level portfolio across stocks, aggregate resulting weights sector-level metrics, construct sector weights (macro), reconcile stock weights sector totals match macro weights. (Micro-level concentration constraints may hold globally reconciliation.)

title: Create a Micro–Macro Allocation Framework (MMAF) portfolio — create_mmaf_portfolio
code: create_mmaf_portfolio(   universe_m_d_ref,   mmaf_method = "bottom_up",   eligible_returns_m_xts_upd_ref = NULL,   covariance_matrix = NULL,   selected_benchmark_m_xts_upd_ref = NULL,   active_returns = if (is.null(selected_benchmark_m_xts_upd_ref)) FALSE else TRUE,   cov_estimation_method = "sample",   cov_matrix_sample_size = if (is.null(eligible_returns_m_xts_upd_ref)) NULL else     nrow(eligible_returns_m_xts_upd_ref),   groups_m_d_ref,   mmaf_group_col,   liquidity_m_d_ref,   top_down_proxy_port_method = "rp",   micro_port_construction_method,   linkage = "single",   liquidity_constraint_policy = NULL,   turnover_constraint_policy = NULL,   concentration_constraint_policy = NULL,   cap_weighting_metric = NULL,   n_random_ports = 2000,   random_ports_method = "sample",   opt_objective = "sharpe",   opt_method = "random",   ridge_pen = NULL,   n_resamples = 0,   exp_ret_score_jitter = 0,   cov_eigval_jitter = 0,   rp_method = "cyclical-spinu",   exp_ret_score_tilt = NULL,   exp_ret_score_tilt_eta = NULL,   macro_port_construction_method,   macro_concentration_constraint_policy = NULL,   macro_cap_weighting_metric = NULL,   macro_n_random_ports = 2000,   macro_random_ports_method = "sample",   macro_opt_objective = "sharpe",   macro_opt_method = "random",   macro_ridge_pen = NULL,   macro_n_resamples = 0,   macro_exp_ret_score_jitter = 0,   macro_cov_eigval_jitter = 0,   macro_rp_method = "cyclical-spinu",   macro_exp_ret_score_tilt = NULL,   macro_exp_ret_score_tilt_eta = NULL,   macro_linkage = "single",   selected_benchmark = NULL,   bench_assets_returns_m_xts_upd_ref = NULL,   lower_quantile_winsorization = 0.025,   upper_quantile_winsorization = 0.975,   parallel = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Create a Micro–Macro Allocation Framework (MMAF) portfolio — create_mmaf_portfolio
text: universe_m_d_ref data.frame/tibble (least) columns: id, tickers, dates, is_eligible, exp_ret_score, optional columns used micro methods (e.g., benchmark/target weights, liquidity features). refer single date. mmaf_method Character scalar: "top_down" "bottom_up". eligible_returns_m_xts_upd_ref optional xts object containing return data eligible tickers, used covariance matrix estimation Risk-Parity MVO methods. covariance_matrix Numeric covariance matrix row/col names exactly match tickers eligible names (ordering enforced). selected_benchmark_m_xts_upd_ref optional xts object containing benchmark returns used compute active returns (active_returns = TRUE). active_returns Logical. TRUE, covariance estimation use active returns (asset returns minus benchmark). Defaults FALSE selected_benchmark_m_xts_upd_ref NULL, otherwise TRUE. cov_estimation_method optional character string specifying method estimating covariance matrix. Defaults NULL. cov_matrix_sample_size Integer. Number time periods (rows eligible_returns_m_xts_upd_ref) used estimate covariance matrix. NULL, uses available observations. groups_m_d_ref optional data frame used group constraints covariance matrix estimation. include group information used. Defaults NULL. mmaf_group_col Character scalar naming column groups_m_d_ref carries group/sector classification. (wrapper enforces 4th column.) liquidity_m_d_ref Optional data.frame/tibble liquidity features aligned id, tickers, dates. provided, group-level liquidity metrics aggregated weighted means intra-sector micro weights. top_down_proxy_port_method Character: micro method build proxy intra-sector portfolios initial pass "top_down" (e.g., "rp", "hrp", "ew"). micro_port_construction_method Character micro method used actual intra-sector optimization (e.g., "mvo", "rp", "hrp", "ew", custom handled set_portfolio_weights()). linkage Character, passed hierarchical methods applicable. liquidity_constraint_policy Optional list describing liquidity caps micro level. turnover_constraint_policy Optional list describing turnover caps micro level. concentration_constraint_policy Optional list e.g. max_abs_active_individual_weight micro level. cap_weighting_metric Optional character capitalization weighting metric (used). n_random_ports Integer, number random portfolios (MVO/random search). random_ports_method Character, sampling method random portfolios. opt_objective Character, optimization objective (e.g., "sharpe"). opt_method Character, optimizer selector MVO/random search. ridge_pen Numeric NULL, ridge penalty MVO (micro). n_resamples Integer, number resamples robust MVO (micro). exp_ret_score_jitter Numeric, jitter expected return score (micro MVO). cov_eigval_jitter Numeric, jitter covariance eigenvalues (micro MVO). rp_method Character, risk parity method micro level (e.g., "cyclical-spinu"). exp_ret_score_tilt Optional numeric vector/column name RP tilt title: Create a Micro–Macro Allocation Framework (MMAF) portfolio — create_mmaf_portfolio
text: list : universe_m_d_ref Input universe joined final weights (stock-level). group_weights Named numeric vector sector weights (macro). macro Return object set_portfolio_weights() macro level. micro Top-: list per-sector micro return objects. Bottom-: single-element list list(consolidated = <micro_object>). group_cov_matrix Sector--sector covariance matrix implied micro solutions.

title: Create a Micro–Macro Allocation Framework (MMAF) portfolio — create_mmaf_portfolio
text: Top-emphasizes sector allocation: sectors first represented proxy intra-sector portfolios estimate sector ER/liquidity sector--sector covariance. optimizing sector weights (macro), intra-sector portfolios re-optimized sector budgets. Constraint policies (concentration/turnover/liquidity) scaled sector budget applicable. Bottom-emphasizes stock selection: single micro portfolio built across names. Sector metrics aggregated solution, macro sector weights computed, final stock weights reconciled match macro sector totals. Micro-level concentration constraints may longer hold exactly reconciliation. function assumes (upstream contract) : universe_m_d_ref / groups_m_d_ref / liquidity_m_d_ref single-date. covariance_matrix row/col names match eligible tickers (ordering). port@universe_m_d_ref@data carries liquidity columns used. mmaf_group_col 4th column groups_m_d_ref (enforced wrapper).

title: Create a Micro–Macro Allocation Framework (MMAF) portfolio — create_mmaf_portfolio
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Minimal sketch (objects must be prepared consistently upstream): res_td <- create_mmaf_portfolio(   universe_m_d_ref = universe_df,   mmaf_method = "top_down",   covariance_matrix = Sigma,   groups_m_d_ref = groups_df,   mmaf_group_col = "sector",   liquidity_m_d_ref = liq_df,   top_down_proxy_port_method = "rp",   micro_port_construction_method = "mvo",   macro_port_construction_method = "hrp",   verbose = TRUE )  res_bu <- create_mmaf_portfolio(   universe_m_d_ref = universe_df,   mmaf_method = "bottom_up",   covariance_matrix = Sigma,   groups_m_d_ref = groups_df,   mmaf_group_col = "sector",   liquidity_m_d_ref = liq_df,   micro_port_construction_method = "mvo",   macro_port_construction_method = "rp",   verbose = TRUE )  # Check reconciliation by sector (example): # dplyr::summarise sum of final weights by sector should match res_td$group_weights } # }

Possible elements list : liquidity_floor_rule: character indicating liquidity classification (e.g., micro_caps, small_caps) used filter stocks. Stocks less liquidity specified liquidity_floor_rule considered ineligible. case generate_box_constraints function, liquidity_constraint_policy can also contain: liquidity_cap_rule lists: One many lists used create upper bounds weights based liquidity classification. list must contain: liquidity_classification: character indicating classification cap. liquidity_cap: numeric value indicating cap (upper bound) stocks liquidity classification. Many liquidity caps might created, case, liquidity_cap_rule must identified number (e.g., liquidity_cap_rule_1, liquidity_cap_rule_2, ). turnover_constraint_policy named list containing objects used build buffer zones apply turnover constraints. element constitute buffer_zone, list three elements: liquidity_classification element: liquidity classification (e.g., "micro_caps", "small_caps") buffer zone. top_assets_quantile_buffer: numeric value indicating buffer value relaxes top_assets_quantile stocks specified liquidity classification. turnover_cap: numeric value specifying turnover cap. Stocks less liquid specified buffer zone signal higher respective buffer quantile considered eligible, even meet liquidity_floor_rule. concentration_constraint_policy named list containing four elements: benchmark: character vector describing benchmark used apply constraint. Must correspondence benchmark_weights_m_d_ref max_abs_active_individual_weight: maximum absolute individual active weights. max_abs_active_group_weight: maximum absolute group active weight used creating group constraints generate_group_constraints. given group eligible stock, one greatest signal automatically promoted. Note , context generate_group_constraints, benchmark_weights_m_d_ref data frame must also supplied. groups_m_d_ref data frame containing columns id, tickers, dates, group classification columns following given classification method. tickers current stock universe must unique correspondence data frame. n_random_ports integer indicating number random portfolios generated. random_ports_method character indicating method used generating random portfolios. Possible values "random" "grid". opt_objective character describing objective maximize order choose best portfolio. One "return (max return)", "risk (min risk)" "sharpe (max sharpe-ratio)" opt_method character describing optimization method used. Higher values increase importance close target portfolio. verbose logical indicating whether print messages execution function.

title: Create a Performance Metrics Data Frame — create_performance_m_df
text: data frame containing performance metrics specified signals. metrics include: ID Information: id, tickers, dates. Return Metrics: Arithmetic mean return, geometric mean return, annualized return. Risk Metrics: Standard deviation, semi deviation, downside deviation, maximum drawdown, . Performance Ratios: Sharpe ratio, Sortino ratio, Calmar ratio, Omega ratio, Rachev ratio, others. Additional Metrics: Average recovery, Hurst index, probabilistic Sharpe ratio, minimum track record.

code: create_port_backtest_config(   chosen_score_metric_and_position = NULL,   eligibility_quantile_range = c(0.9, 1),   min_eligible_assets_fallback = NULL,   chosen_scaler = NULL,   scaler_shrinkage = NULL,   use_raw_for_eligibility = NULL,   enable_group_representativeness = NULL,   selected_benchmark = NULL,   initial_buffer_period,   rebalancing_months,   cov_est_method = NULL,   port_construction_method = "ew",   mvo_parameters = NULL,   rp_parameters = NULL,   hrp_parameters = NULL,   mmaf_parameters = NULL,   main_liquidity_metric,   liquidity_floor_cutoffs = NULL,   liquidity_constraint_policy = NULL,   turnover_constraint_policy = NULL,   concentration_constraint_policy = NULL,   transaction_costs_parameters = NULL,   config_name = "not_identified" )

title: Create port_backtest_config Object — create_port_backtest_config
text: chosen_score_metric_and_position object (named vector) specifying expected return score metric associated position. Required sb_backtest_results provided. eligibility_quantile_range numeric vector length 2 (e.g., c(0.9, 1.0)) specifying quantile range used determine eligible assets. min_eligible_assets_fallback numeric value indicating minimum number eligible assets include portfolio. chosen_scaler object class scaler specifying scaling variable applied scores. scaler_shrinkage numeric value 0 1 indicating shrinkage intensity scaler. use_raw_for_eligibility logical value indicating whether use raw scores determining eligibility. enable_group_representativeness Logical; TRUE, ensures least one asset groups groups_m_d_ref selected_benchmark character string indicating benchmark use benchmark-relative backtests. initial_buffer_period numeric value indicating number initial dates skip starting backtest. rebalancing_months numeric vector (e.g., c(3,6,9,12)) indicating months portfolio rebalanced. cov_est_method cov_est_method object specifying covariance estimation method parameters. provided, default created using method "sample" sample size 252; active_returns set TRUE (cov_matrix_benchmark = selected_benchmark) selected_benchmark supplied, FALSE otherwise. port_construction_method character string representing portfolio construction method. Must one "ew" (equal-weight), "sw" (signal-weight), "cw" (cap-weight), "cs" (cap-scaled), "rp" (risk parity), "hrp" (hierarchical risk parity), "mvo" (mean-variance optimization), "mmaf" (micro-macro allocation framework). "custom_weights" supported constructor. mvo_parameters object class mvo_parameters mean-variance optimization. required port_construction_method "mvo". missing port_construction_method "mvo", default created. rp_parameters object class rp_parameters risk parity portfolios. required port_construction_method "rp". missing port_construction_method "rp", default created. hrp_parameters object class hrp_parameters hierarchical risk parity portfolios. required port_construction_method "hrp". missing port_construction_method "hrp", default created. mmaf_parameters object class mmaf_parameters micro-macro allocation framework portfolios. required port_construction_method "mmaf". missing port_construction_method "mmaf", default created (enable_group_representativeness defaults TRUE). main_liquidity_metric character string indicating liquidity metric (.e. column liquidity_m_df) use. liquidity_floor_cutoffs object (e.g., data frame) containing liquidity cutoff values. liquidity_constraint_policy object class liquidity_constraint_policy (optional). turnover_constraint_policy object class turnover_constraint_policy (optional). concentration_constraint_policy object class concentration_constraint_policy (optional). transaction_costs_parameters object specifying transaction cost parameters (optional). config_name character string representing name configuration.

title: Create port_backtest_config Object — create_port_backtest_config

title: Create port_backtest_config Object — create_port_backtest_config
code: # Minimal equal-weighted configuration driven by a single characteristic signal # (a book-yield tilt), rebalanced semi-annually after a 12-period buffer. config <- create_port_backtest_config(   chosen_score_metric_and_position = c(book_yield = "long"),   eligibility_quantile_range = c(0.8, 1.0),   initial_buffer_period = 12,   rebalancing_months = c(6, 12),   main_liquidity_metric = "mean_volfin_3m",   port_construction_method = "ew",   config_name = "ew_book_yield" )  # Benchmark-relative risk-parity configuration: supplying a selected_benchmark # makes the default cov_est_method use active returns against that benchmark. rp_config <- create_port_backtest_config(   chosen_score_metric_and_position = c(book_yield = "long"),   selected_benchmark = "ibov",   initial_buffer_period = 12,   rebalancing_months = 12,   main_liquidity_metric = "mean_volfin_3m",   port_construction_method = "rp",   config_name = "rp_book_yield" )

title: Create sb_backtest_config Object — create_sb_backtest_config
text: sb_algorithm Character string specifying signal-blending algorithm. One 'ols' (default), 'glmnet', 'rf', 'xgb', 'nn' (ML), 'ew', 'sw', 'rp', 'hrp', 'mvo', 'mmaf', 'custom_weights' (heuristic/portfolio). target_fwd_name Name target variable target_m_df. tuning_strategy object class tuning_strategy, specifying strategy tuning hyperparameters. training_sample_size Number observations include training sample. rebalancing_months Months (numeric) model rebalanced (refit). split_method Character string indicating data splitting method ('expanding' 'rolling'). chosen_signals_and_positions named vector chosen signals positions ('long'/'short'). Defaults NULL, coerced '' (use every signal features_m_df). custom_objective Character string, NULL auto-set. ML algorithms: 'squared_error' (default), 'pseudo_huber_error' 'absolute_error' (last two 'xgb'/'nn'). heuristic portfolio algorithms ('sw', 'rp', 'hrp', 'mvo', 'mmaf'): 'max_'/'min_' + heuristic-metric string (defaults 'max_info_ratio' NULL). See display_valid_custom_objectives(). keras_architecture_parameters object class keras_architecture_parameters providing parameters specific keras-based neural networks. signal_port_parameters object class signal_port_parameters, specifying parameters constructing signal portfolios (portfolio-blending). quantile_tau Numeric value indicating tau parameter used quantile regression, 0 1. huber_delta Numeric value greater 0, specifying delta parameter Huber loss function. config_name Name backtest configuration.

title: Create a tickers_catalog Object — create_tickers_catalog
text: raw_features_m_df raw_features_m_df (source panel). date_first_quote data.frame columns tickers date_first_quote (coercible Date). date_last_quote data.frame columns tickers date_last_quote (coercible Date). ... Additional arguments (currently unused). n_days_tolerance Numeric scalar (days). Window used decide whether last-quote date indicates delisting relative raw_features_m_df@current_date. Default: 10.

title: Create a tickers_catalog Object — create_tickers_catalog
text: object class tickers_catalog. object class tickers_catalog slots: catalog, tickers, perm_id (named tickers), tickers_first_quote, tickers_last_quote, untraded, delisted, listed, old, current_date, meta_dataframe_name, n_days_tolerance, ticker_change_history.

title: Create a tickers_catalog Object — create_tickers_catalog
text: generic performs responsibilities (method implementations perform checks described ): Validate input types required columns date_first_quote date_last_quote. Verify tickers identical across raw_features_m_df, date_first_quote date_last_quote. Enforce date_last_quote >= date_first_quote present (NAs allowed). Generate deterministic perm_id per ticker ticker + date_first_quote. Extract current_date recent date raw_features_m_df@data$dates. Classify tickers untraded (dates NA), delisted (last_quote < current_date - tolerance), listed (last_quote >= current_date - tolerance). Emit informative errors warnings duplicate tickers, mismatched ticker sets, inconsistent NA patterns, invalid date ordering, tradable stocks found. Method behavior validations (errors/warnings mirror unit tests): Requires date tables contain columns tickers + date_first_quote / date_last_quote; otherwise errors "date_first_quote must columns 'tickers' 'date_first_quote', date_last_quote must 'tickers' 'date_last_quote'." Converts date columns Date errors duplicate tickers ("Duplicate tickers found date_first_quote date_last_quote."). Errors "tradable stocks identified" dates NA. Requires ticker sets match across inputs; otherwise errors "Mismatch tickers raw_features_m_df, date_first_quote, date_last_quote." Enforces pairwise NA-ness (NA neither) errors "date_first_quote date_last_quote must NA neither." Warns modal date_last_quote older current_date - n_days_tolerance : "common date date_last_quote last date raw_features_m_df - n_days_tolerance. Consider increasing n_days_tolerance" Errors date_last_quote < date_first_quote : "date_last_quote must greater equal date_first_quote tickers." Warns date_last_quote > current_date: "date_last_quote values greater current_date. may indicate future dates errors data." Generates deterministic perm_id values using MD5-derived short hash ticker + first_quote (NA first-quote uses "NA"), sorts internal catalog perm_id, classifies tickers untraded, delisted, listed, old.

title: Create a tickers_catalog Object — create_tickers_catalog
text: create_tickers_catalog(   raw_features_m_df = raw_features_m_df,   date_first_quote = data.frame,   date_last_quote = data.frame ): Method builds tickers_catalog raw_features_m_df two data.frames containing per-ticker first- last-quote dates.

title: Define Signal Eligibility — define_signal_eligibility
text: function evaluates eligibility signals inclusion investment universe based various performance metrics statistical adjustments. performs initial data checks, computes performance metrics, applies statistical adjustments (frequentist Bayesian), classifies signals according specified selection policy.

title: Define Signal Eligibility — define_signal_eligibility
code: define_signal_eligibility(   selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref,   selected_market_factor_proxy_m_xts_upd_ref,   custom_signal_universe_metrics_m_upd_ref = NULL,   p_correction_method = "none",   signal_significance_threshold = 0.05,   enable_theme_representativeness = TRUE,   model_structure = "no_pooled",   theme_level_intercept = NULL,   theme_level_slope = NULL,   lmer_control = list(lmer_optimizer = "nloptwrap", lmer_optimization_objective = "REML",     hierarchical_p_value_method = "Satterthwaite"),   active_returns = TRUE,   priors_m_upd_ref = NULL,   user_priors = NULL,   brms_control = list(iter = 2000, chains = 4, thin = 1, seed = NA, adapt_delta = 0.99),   prior_derivation_control = list(half_t_df = 30),   selected_signal_themes_m_d_ref,   lower_quantile_winsorization = 0.025,   upper_quantile_winsorization = 0.975,   verbose = TRUE,   parallel = TRUE )

title: Define Signal Eligibility — define_signal_eligibility
text: selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref xts object containing backtest returns various signals. selected_market_factor_proxy_m_xts_upd_ref xts object containing benchmark returns data. first column dates, subsequent columns contain returns. custom_signal_universe_metrics_m_upd_ref Optional meta-dataframe custom metrics signals, precomputed alpha, IR, etc. p_correction_method method p-value correction. Possible options : "none": correction. "bayesian": Bayesian hierarchical model (via brms) used adjustment. "bonferroni", "holm", "hochberg", "hommel": FWER-controlling methods. "BH", "fdr", "": FDR-controlling methods. signal_significance_threshold numeric value alpha significance level. set 1, signals valid alphas selected. Signals retained CAPM alpha statistically significant. enable_theme_representativeness Logical. TRUE, ensures least one signal per theme selected, even signal passes significance threshold, choosing signal highest alpha t-stat. model_structure character string, either "partial_pooled" "no_pooled", indicating type model structure use mixed-effects estimation. theme_level_intercept string specifying intercept structure theme level: one "random", "theme_specific", "fixed". relevant model_structure == "partial_pooled"; combined theme_level_slope build model_spec_theme_level (see Model Specifications Theme Level ). theme_level_slope string specifying slope structure theme level: one "fixed" "theme_specific". relevant model_structure == "partial_pooled". lmer_control list arguments passed lme4::lmer. Expected components: lmer_optimizer: Optimization algorithm used (e.g., "nloptwrap", "bobyqa", "Nelder_Mead", "nlminbwrap"). lmer_optimization_objective: Either "REML" "ML". hierarchical_p_value_method: p-value calculation method (e.g., "Satterthwaite"). active_returns Logical. TRUE, returns adjusted subtracting benchmark returns computing performance metrics. priors_m_upd_ref meta-dataframe used derive informative priors via derive_informative_priors_from_data(). Must include columns: id, tickers, dates, return, market_factor_proxy, theme. provided user_priors set. user_priors list brms::set_prior() objects manually defined user. used priors_m_upd_ref NULL. brms_control list parameters control Bayesian model fitting brms::brm: chains: Number MCMC chains (default 4). iter: Total iterations per chain (default 2000). warmup: Warmup iterations per chain (default = floor(iter / 2)). thin: Thinning interval (default 1). seed: Random seed (default NA). adapt_delta: Target acceptance rate HMC sampler (default 0.99). prior_derivation_control list additional parameters prior generation: half_t_df: Degrees freedom half-t distribution scale parameters (default 30). selected_signal_themes_m_d_ref meta-dataframe columns id, dates, theme. Specifies group membership signal. include data current date . lower_quantile_winsorization Numeric 0 1. Lower quantile threshold winsorization signal metrics (e.g., alpha t-stats). upper_quantile_winsorization Numeric 0 1. Upper quantile threshold winsorization signal metrics. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints messages console. parallel Logical. Enables parallel execution (Bayesian models).

title: Define Signal Eligibility — define_signal_eligibility
text: list following components: signal_universe_m_d_ref dataframe computed performance metrics, adjusted p-values, classification flags. frequentist_results Results frequentist mixed-effects model (applicable). bayesian_results Results Bayesian model (applicable).

title: Define Signal Eligibility — define_signal_eligibility
text: priors_m_upd_ref provided, function uses frequentist mixed-effects model derive priors, later used Bayesian model: Priors location parameters (e.g., alpha, beta) follow normal distributions. Priors scale parameters (e.g., random effect std. devs) follow half-t distributions. Correlation priors follow LKJ distribution.

title: Define Signal Eligibility — define_signal_eligibility
text: model_spec_theme_level built paste0(theme_level_intercept, "_intercept_", theme_level_slope, "_slope") must resolve one : random_intercept_fixed_slope Random intercepts theme level, fixed (global) slope; random intercepts slopes theme-signal level. theme_specific_intercept_fixed_slope Fixed intercepts theme, fixed (global) slope; random intercepts slopes theme-signal level. theme_specific_intercept_theme_specific_slope Fixed intercepts slopes theme; random intercepts slopes theme-signal level. fixed_intercept_fixed_slope single fixed (global) intercept slope; random intercepts slopes theme-signal level (theme-level effect).

what: Derive Informative Priors from Data — derive_informative_priors_from_data
title: Derive Informative Priors from Data — derive_informative_priors_from_data
text: function fits frequentist hierarchical linear mixed-effects model using lme4 package, based input data specified model structure. resulting parameter estimates used derive informative priors Bayesian modeling using brms package.

title: Derive Informative Priors from Data — derive_informative_priors_from_data
code: derive_informative_priors_from_data(   priors_m_upd_ref,   model_spec_theme_level,   half_t_df = 30,   lmer_optimizer = "nloptwrap",   lmer_optimization_objective = "REML" )

title: Derive Informative Priors from Data — derive_informative_priors_from_data
text: priors_m_upd_ref (meta)data frame containing following columns: id: Identifier observation. tickers: tickers corresponding individual securities instruments. dates: Date observation. return: Return signal date. market_factor_proxy: Market factor proxy return date. theme: Theme associated signal, used clustering hierarchical Bayesian model (e.g., "value", "growth"). ticker must uniquely linked single theme. data frame include observations current date. model_spec_theme_level character string indicating structure hierarchical Bayesian model. parameter controls specification parameters theme level, assuming tickers uniquely nested within theme. Options include: "random_intercept_fixed_slope": Random effects theme-level intercept, fixed (global) slope. Includes random intercepts themes random intercepts slopes theme-signal combination. "theme_specific_intercept_fixed_slope": Fixed intercepts theme, global slope market factor proxy. Nested variability within themes modeled using random intercepts slopes theme-signal combinations. "theme_specific_intercept_theme_specific_slope": Fixed intercepts slopes theme, via interaction terms themes market factor proxy, plus random intercepts slopes theme-signal combinations. "fixed_intercept_fixed_slope": single fixed (global) intercept slope, random intercepts slopes theme-signal combinations (theme-level fixed random intercept/slope). half_t_df numeric indicating degrees freedom half-t distribution applied model random effects. Although function specifies regular student t distribution, brms use half-t distribution, ensuring strictly positive parameters. lmer_optimizer character string specifying optimizer used lme4::lmer function. passed lme4::lmerControl, used lme4::lmer function. Options include: 'nloptwrap', 'bobyqa', 'Nelder_Mead' 'nlminbwrap' lmer_optimization_objective character string indicating whether estimates chosen optimize 'REML' criterion 'likelihood'.

title: Derive Informative Priors from Data — derive_informative_priors_from_data
text: list two components: priors: list brms::set_prior objects specifying derived priors. model: fitted linear mixed-effects model (lme4::lmer object).

title: Derive Informative Priors from Data — derive_informative_priors_from_data
text: function uses frequentist linear mixed-effects models estimate parameters subsequently translated Bayesian priors: Priors location parameters (e.g., intercepts, slopes) follow normal distribution. Priors scale parameters (e.g., random effect standard deviations) follow half-t distribution. Correlation priors random effects modeled using LKJ (Lewandowski-Kurowicka-Joe) distribution.

title: Derive Informative Priors from Data — derive_informative_priors_from_data
text: model includes: Fixed intercept slope market factor proxy. Random intercepts theme level. Random intercepts slopes theme-signal combination. model equation : $$y_i = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x_i + b_{0,t_i} + b_{0,g_i} + b_{1,g_i} \cdot x_i + \epsilon_i$$ See detailed breakdown example section.

title: Derive Informative Priors from Data — derive_informative_priors_from_data
text: model includes: Fixed intercepts theme, expressed summation themes. global fixed slope market factor proxy. Random intercepts slopes theme-signal combinations. model equation : $$y_{} = \sum_{k} \beta_{k} \cdot \text{theme}_{k,} + \beta_{m} \cdot x_{} + b_{0,g_{}} + b_{1,g_{}} \cdot x_{} + \epsilon_{}$$

title: Derive Informative Priors from Data — derive_informative_priors_from_data
text: model includes: Fixed intercepts slopes theme. Interaction terms themes market factor proxy (separate global slope term). Random intercepts slopes theme-signal combinations. model equation : $$y_{} = \sum_k \beta_k \cdot \text{theme}_{k,} + \sum_k \gamma_k \cdot \text{theme}_{k,} \cdot x_{} + b_{0,g_{}} + b_{1,g_{}} \cdot x_{} + \epsilon_{}$$

title: Derive Informative Priors from Data — derive_informative_priors_from_data
text: model includes: single fixed (global) intercept slope market factor proxy (theme-level fixed effect). Random intercepts slopes theme-signal combinations. model equation : $$y_{} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x_{} + b_{0,g_{}} + b_{1,g_{}} \cdot x_{} + \epsilon_{}$$

title: Display Eligibility Criteria with Colors — display_eligibility_criteria
code: display_eligibility_criteria()

title: Display Eligibility Criteria with Colors — display_eligibility_criteria
text: Prints colored eligibility criteria console.

title: Display Eligibility Criteria with Colors — display_eligibility_criteria
code: display_eligibility_criteria() #> Classify the universe based on signals and other custom and user-defined rules. #>  #> The eligibility of a stock/signal portfolio depends on a series of criteria, as explained in Details. Default behavior is to apply only the Only Top Assets Rule, in which case assets are promoted based on their signal being above a given quantile. #>  #> The function provides additional custom rules and also accepts user-defined rules. #>  #> ## Eligibility Criteria #> To be promoted as eligible, assets must meet one of the following criteria: #>  #> 1. Regular Eligibility #>    - Only Top Assets Rule: Asset must be in the top quantile as specified by top_quantile. #>      - To ignore this behavior, set top_quantile to 0. #>    - Liquidity Floor Rule (exclusive for stocks): must meet minimum liquidity requirements as defined by the liquidity floor rule. #>  #> 2. OR Active Weights Constraint Policy Eligibility: #>    - Maximum Absolute Individual Active Weight Rule: Benchmark weight must exceed the maximum absolute individual active weight threshold. #>  #> 3. OR Turnover Policy Eligibility: (exclusive for stocks) #>    - Stock must be in one of the buffer zones. For this to happen: #>      - Stock must be in the top quantile buffer (signal >= top_quantile_buffer). #>      - Stock must be in the pre-rebalancing portfolio. #>      - Stock must meet the liquidity classification of the buffer zone. #>  #> 4. OR user_defined_OR_rules Eligibility #>  #> 5. OR Group Representativeness Eligibility: #>    - If there are no stocks or signal portfolios in one of the groups specified in concentration_constraint_policy, a representative will be included according to the best quantile. #>  #> 6. AND user_defined_AND_rules #>  #> ## Dominance of Rules #> - The Active Weights Constraint Policy Eligibility is dominant; assets meeting this rule will always be eligible. #> - The Turnover Policy Eligibility takes precedence over the Liquidity Floor Rule; thus, a stock in the buffer zone will be included even if the liquidity floor rule suggests otherwise. #> - Assets that meet user_defined_OR_rules will always be promoted. #> - Assets that fail to meet user_defined_AND_rules will always be excluded.

title: Display Valid Custom Objectives — display_valid_custom_objectives

title: Display Valid Custom Objectives — display_valid_custom_objectives

title: Display Valid Custom Objectives — display_valid_custom_objectives
text: Prints valid heuristic SB metrics console, categorizing needed.

title: Display Valid Custom Objectives — display_valid_custom_objectives
text: Metrics containing either "act_", "info_ratio", "track_err" active performance metrics, used active_returns slot sb_backtest_config TRUE. Metrics "posterior" relate Bayesian correction method p_correction_method slot set bayesian. Raw return metrics used active_returns FALSE. Frequentist CAPM metrics apply p_correction_method bayesian. Additionally, error metrics 'squared_error', 'pseudo_huber_error', 'absolute_error' valid sb_algorithm either xgb nn.

title: Display Valid Custom Objectives — display_valid_custom_objectives
code: display_valid_custom_objectives() #> Valid Heuristic SB Metrics List #>  #> ## Required Metrics for sb_algorithm 'sw' or 'markowitz' #> These metrics are needed when the `sb_algorithm` slot in the `sb_backtest_config` class is set to either 'sw' or 'markowitz'. #>  #> ### Active Performance Metrics #> The following metrics are considered active performance metrics and are used when the `active_returns` slot of the `ss_backtest_config` object is TRUE. These metrics include any metrics containing 'act_', 'info_ratio', or 'track_err'. #>  #> 1. Active Arithmetic Mean Return   - act_arith_mean_ret #> 2. Active Geometric Mean Return   - act_geom_mean_ret #> 3. Active Annualized Return   - act_ann_ret #> 4. Tracking Error   - track_err #> 5. Annualized Tracking Error   - ann_track_err #> 6. Active Semi Deviation   - act_semi_dev #> 7. Active Downside Deviation   - act_down_dev #> 8. Active Drawdown Deviation   - act_dd_dev #> 9. Active Downside Frequency   - act_down_freq #> 10. Active Expected Shortfall   - act_exp_short #> 11. Active Pain   - act_pain #> 12. Active Ulcer   - act_ulcer #> 13. Active Maximum Drawdown   - act_max_dd #> 14. Active Skewness   - act_skew #> 15. Active Kurtosis   - act_kurt #> 16. Information Ratio   - info_ratio #> 17. Annualized Information Ratio   - ann_info_ratio #> 18. Information Ratio (Semi Deviation)   - info_ratio_semi_dev #> 19. Active Sortino Ratio   - act_sortino_ratio #> 20. Active Annualized Burke Ratio   - act_ann_burke_ratio #> 21. Active Inverted D-Ratio   - act_inv_d_ratio #> 22. Information Ratio (Expected Shortfall)   - info_ratio_exp_short #> 23. Active Annualized Pain Ratio   - act_ann_pain_ratio #> 24. Active Annualized Martin Ratio   - act_ann_martin_ratio #> 25. Active Annualized Calmar Ratio   - act_ann_calmar_ratio #> 26. Active Omega Ratio   - act_omega #> 27. Active Rachev Ratio   - act_rachev_ratio #> 28. Active Average Drawdown Recovery   - act_avg_dd_rec #> 29. Active Average Drawdown Length   - act_avg_dd_length #> 30. Active Hurst Index   - act_hurst #> 31. Active Minimum Track Record   - act_min_track_record #> 32. Probabilistic Info Ratio   - prob_info_ratio #> 33. Active Modigliani Ratio   - act_modigliani #> 34. Active Annualized Modigliani Ratio   - act_ann_modigliani #>  #> ### Raw Performance Metrics #> The following metrics are used when the `active_returns` slot of the `ss_backtest_config` object is FALSE. #>  #> 1. Arithmetic Mean Return   - arith_mean_ret #> 2. Geometric Mean Return   - geom_mean_ret #> 3. Annualized Return   - ann_ret #> 4. Standard Deviation   - std_dev #> 5. Annualized Standard Deviation   - ann_std_dev #> 6. Semi Deviation   - semi_dev #> 7. Downside Deviation   - down_dev #> 8. Drawdown Deviation   - dd_dev #> 9. Downside Frequency   - down_freq #> 10. Expected Shortfall   - exp_short #> 11. Pain   - pain #> 12. Ulcer   - ulcer #> 13. Maximum Drawdown   - max_dd #> 14. Skewness   - skew #> 15. Kurtosis   - kurt #> 16. Sharpe Ratio   - sharpe_ratio #> 17. Annualized Sharpe Ratio   - ann_sharpe_ratio #> 18. Sortino Ratio   - sortino_ratio #> 19. Annualized Burke Ratio   - ann_burke_ratio #> 20. Inverted D-Ratio   - inv_d_ratio #> 21. Omega Ratio   - omega #> 22. Rachev Ratio   - rachev_ratio #> 23. Average Drawdown Recovery   - avg_dd_rec #> 24. Average Drawdown Length   - avg_dd_length #> 25. Hurst Index   - hurst #> 26. Minimum Track Record   - min_track_record #> 27. Modigliani Ratio   - modigliani #> 28. Annualized Modigliani Ratio   - ann_modigliani #>  #> ### Frequentist CAPM Metrics #> The following metrics are used when the `p_correction_method` slot of the `ss_backtest_config` object is NOT `bayesian`. #>     Therefore, these metrics include the Bayesian metrics without the 'posterior' prefix. #>     When the 'model_stucture' in ss_backtest_config is 'partial_pooled', one can choose between 'theme_alpha', 'individual_alpha', #>     'theme_beta' and 'individual_beta'. When the 'model_stucture' in ss_backtest_config is 'no_pooled', one can only choose between #>     'alpha' and 'beta'. #>  #> 1. Theme Alpha   - theme_alpha #> 2. Individual Alpha   - individual_alpha #> 3. Alpha Standard Error   - alpha_se #> 4. Theme Beta   - theme_beta #> 5. Individual Beta   - individual_beta #> 6. Specific Risk   - specific_risk #> 7. Alpha T-Statistic   - alpha_t_stat #> 8. Treynor Ratio   - treynor_ratio #> 9. Appraisal Ratio   - appraisal_ratio #>  #> ### For no_pooled model_structures, the theme and individual alpha and beta are just 'alpha' and 'beta'. The remaining metrics stay the same:  #> 10. Alpha   - alpha #> 11. Beta   - beta #>  #> ### Posterior Metrics (Bayesian Correction Method) #> The following metrics are used when the `p_correction_method` slot of the `sb_backtest_config` is set to `bayesian`. #>  #> 1. Posterior Theme Alpha   - posterior_theme_alpha #> 2. Posterior Individual Alpha   - posterior_individual_alpha #> 3. Posterior Alpha Standard Error   - posterior_alpha_se #> 4. Posterior Theme Beta   - posterior_theme_beta #> 5. Posterior Individual Beta   - posterior_individual_beta #> 6. Posterior Specific Risk   - posterior_specific_risk #> 7. Posterior Alpha T-Statistic   - posterior_alpha_t_stat #> 8. Posterior Treynor Ratio   - posterior_treynor_ratio #> 9. Posterior Appraisal Ratio   - posterior_appraisal_ratio #>  #> ### Error Metrics for XGBoost or Neural Networks (sb_algorithm = 'xgb' or 'nn') #> These error metrics are valid only when the `sb_algorithm` is set to 'xgb' or 'nn'. #>  #> 1. Squared Error - squared_error #> 2. Pseudo-Huber Error - pseudo_huber_error #> 3. Absolute Error - absolute_error #> ## Additionally, user-defined metrics can also be considered, if they have been passed to run_ss_backtest through a custom_signal_universe_metrics_m_df object #>

what: factoRverse: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals — factoRverse-package
title: factoRverse: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals — factoRverse-package
text: end--end toolkit factor-investing research deployment. provides point--time construction firm-level characteristics anomalies, look-ahead-safe preprocessing, consistent, auditable interface across four workflows: characteristic-sorted portfolio backtesting; signal selection multiple-testing control (family-wise error rate false discovery rate procedures, hierarchical Bayesian partial pooling); signal blending heuristics machine-learning models (elastic net, random forests, gradient boosting neural networks) unified tuning walk-forward scheme; risk-based portfolio construction (equal weight, risk parity, hierarchical risk parity, mean-variance optimization) liquidity, turnover concentration constraints transaction costs.

title: factoRverse: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals — factoRverse-package
text: factoRverse organises factor-investing pipeline four consistent config + data objects -> run_*() -> *_results workflows: Characteristic portfolios: rank stocks signal backtest top-quantile, long-portfolios run_port_backtest(). Signal selection: control factor zoo multiple-testing hierarchical Bayesian methods via run_ss_backtest(). Signal blending: combine selected signals heuristics machine learning via run_sb_backtest(). Deployment: feed blended score back run_port_backtest() build final, constraint- cost-aware book. Data enters create_meta_dataframe() / create_meta_xts() engineered compute_*() family map_recipe_timewise().

title: factoRverse: Portfolio Construction with ML-Based and Characteristics-Based Signals — factoRverse-package
text: Maintainer: Paulo Guimaraes paulo.guimaraes871@gmail.com (ORCID)

text: signal_universe_m_d_ref data frame containing signal universe. provided, data object updated posteriors. selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref xts containing backtest returns data various signals. selected_market_factor_proxy_m_xts_upd_ref numeric vector containing benchmark returns data. vector recycled match length backtest returns data. selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_upd_ref already processed selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref. data.frame already long format contemplates selected_market_factor_proxy_m_xts_upd_ref selected_signal_themes_m_d_ref theme data. elected_priors object class brmsprior containing priors Bayesian model, generated derive_informative_priors_from_data function provided directly user. selected_signal_themes_m_d_ref data frame containing metadata signals. data frame include: tickers: Signal identifiers matching selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref. theme: Group membership signal, defining clusters Bayesian hierarchical model. dates: Dates corresponding backtest data. input ensures proper alignment signals associated themes. model_spec_theme_level character string specifying desired Bayesian model structure. title: Fit a Bayesian Hierarchical Regression Model and Summarize Posterior Metrics — fit_bayesian_hierarchical_model
text: function performs following steps: Preprocessing: Converts backtest returns data long format, merges metadata signals themes, adds market factor proxy. Formula Specification: Defines Bayesian model formula based chosen model_spec_theme_level. Model Fitting: Fits Bayesian model using brm function brms package, incorporating provided priors prepared data. Posterior Summarization: Extracts posterior draws computes signal-level metrics alphas, betas, sigmas, active returns, tracking errors, information ratios (IR), appraisal ratios (AP), Treynor ratios. Updates signal_universe_m_d_ref data frame metrics. hierarchical approach allows model capture global (theme-level) local (signal-level) effects leveraging flexibility Bayesian regression. inclusion posterior metrics enables deeper performance analysis signals.

title: Fit Signal Blending (SB) Model — fit_sb_model
text: Fits Signal Blending (SB) model based specified sb_algorithm, preparing training model given data, hyperparameters, constraints. function dispatches various modeling workflows, including OLS, GLMNET, Ranger (RF), XGBoost, Keras (NN), heuristic portfolios (equal- signal-weighted, custom weights), Risk Parity (RP), Hierarchical Risk Parity (HRP), Mean-Variance Optimization (MVO), Micro-Macro Allocation Framework (MMAF), depending input.

title: Fit Signal Blending (SB) Model — fit_sb_model
text: sb_algorithm character specifying signal blending algorithm. Options include: "ols", "glmnet", "rf", "xgb", "nn", "ew", "sw", "rp", "hrp", "mvo", "mmaf", "custom_weights". target_fwd_name character indicating target variable's name. selected_features_corrected_positions_m_refit matrix dataframe containing features model refitting. target_m_refit vector containing target variable model refitting. selected_full_data_corrected_positions_m_refit_clean cleaned meta-dataframe refitting model. custom_objective_translated character specifying custom objective function optimization. huber_delta numeric value specifying delta parameter Huber loss (used XGBoost NN). quantile_tau numeric value specifying quantile level (used custom objectives). early_stop numeric value specifying early stopping criteria (applicable). keras_architecture_parameters list containing Keras neural network architecture specifications. optimal_hyper named list optimal hyperparameters specified sb_algorithm. chosen_eval_metric_translated character specifying evaluation metric validation. most_recent_signal_universe_m_d_ref meta-dataframe representing recent signal universe. most_recent_custom_signal_weights_m_d_ref meta-dataframe containing custom signal weights. selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref xts object containing backtested returns corrected positions. cov_matrix_sample_size numeric value specifying sample size covariance matrix estimation. cov_estimation_method character specifying method covariance estimation (e.g., "sample"). active_returns logical value indicating whether use active returns (default: TRUE). selected_cov_matrix_benchmark_m_xts_upd_ref xts object representing selected market factor proxy. groups_m_d_ref meta-dataframe containing group information assets. bench_assets_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref 'xts' object containg returns data selected_benchmark. used heuristics methods selected_benchmark character vector indicating selected benchmark tickers. used heuristics methods rp_method character specifying method Risk Parity optimization. exp_ret_score_tilt Character argument specififying whether tilt must applied risk-parity weights exp_ret_score_tilt_eta Numeric. intensity tilt effect using exp_ret_score_tilt. Higher values increase tilt effect. linkage Character. Linkage method hierarchical clustering Risk Parity. Defaults "single". n_random_ports numeric value specifying number random portfolios generate (MVO). random_ports_method character specifying method generating random portfolios. opt_objective character specifying optimization objective (e.g., "sharpe"). opt_method character specifying optimization method (e.g., "random"). ridge_pen Numeric. Ridge penalty MVO optimization improve numerical stability. Defaults NULL. n_resamples Number resamples resampled MVO. Defaults 0. exp_ret_score_jitter Numeric. Standard deviation jitter added expected return scores resampling. Defaults 0. cov_eigval_jitter Numeric. Standard deviation jitter added covariance matrix eigenvalues resampling. Defaults 0. target_port_m_d_ref Optional. data frame containing columns id, tickers, dates, target portfolio weights. concentration_constraint_policy policy object defining concentration constraints. mmaf_method Character. Method Micro-Macro Allocation Framework. Options "bottom_up" "top_down". Defaults "bottom_up". top_down_proxy_port_method Character. Method constructing top-proxy portfolio MMAF. Options "ew", "sw", "rp", "mvo". mmaf_group_col Character. Column name groups_m_d_ref used define groups MMAF. micro_port_construction_method Character micro method used allocate within groups (e.g., "ew", "rp", "hrp", "mvo"). macro_port_construction_method Character macro method used allocate across groups (e.g., "ew", "rp", "hrp", "mvo"). strictly neutral top-sector allocation, prefer "ew", "rp" "hrp" keep macro_exp_ret_score_tilt = NULL. macro_concentration_constraint_policy Optional list group-level weight caps. macro_n_random_ports Integer, number random portfolios macro level. macro_random_ports_method Character, sampling method (macro). macro_opt_objective Character, optimization objective macro. macro_opt_method Character, optimizer selector macro. macro_ridge_pen Numeric NULL, ridge penalty macro MVO. macro_n_resamples Integer, number resamples macro MVO. macro_exp_ret_score_jitter Numeric, jitter sector ER (macro MVO). macro_cov_eigval_jitter Numeric, jitter macro covariance eigenvalues. macro_rp_method Character, risk parity method macro. macro_exp_ret_score_tilt Optional numeric vector/column name RP tilt (macro). macro_exp_ret_score_tilt_eta Optional numeric, tilt intensity macro RP. macro_linkage Character, passed macro hierarchical methods applicable. upper_quantile_winsorization numeric value specifying upper winsorization quantile. lower_quantile_winsorization numeric value specifying lower winsorization quantile. verbose logical value indicating whether enable verbose output model training.

text: universe_m_d_ref dataframe assets tickers, is_eligible, final_signal columns also generated application constraints policies classify_investment_universe liquidity_constraint_policy Optional. named list containing objects used apply liquidity constraints. Possible elements list : liquidity_floor_rule: character indicating liquidity classification (e.g., micro_caps, small_caps) used filter stocks. Stocks less liquidity specified liquidity_floor_rule considered ineligible. case generate_box_constraints function, liquidity_constraint_policy can also contain: liquidity_cap_rule lists: One many lists used create upper bounds weights based liquidity classification. list must contain: liquidity_classification: character indicating classification cap. liquidity_cap: numeric value indicating cap (upper bound) stocks liquidity classification. Many liquidity caps might created, case, liquidity_cap_rule must identified number (e.g., liquidity_cap_rule_1, liquidity_cap_rule_2, ). turnover_constraint_policy named list containing objects used build buffer zones apply turnover constraints. element constitute buffer_zone, list three elements: liquidity_classification element: liquidity classification (e.g., "micro_caps", "small_caps") buffer zone. top_stock_quantile_buffer: numeric value indicating buffer value relaxes top_stocks_quantile stocks specified liquidity classification. turnover_cap: numeric value specifying turnover cap. Stocks less liquid specified buffer zone signal higher respective buffer quantile considered eligible, even meet liquidity_floor_rule. concentration_constraint_policy named list containing four elements: benchmark: character vector describing benchmark used apply constraint. Must correspondence benchmark_weights_m_d_ref max_abs_active_individual_weight: maximum absolute individual active weights. max_abs_active_sector_weight: maximum absolute sector active weight used creating group constraints generate_sector_constraints. given sector eligible stock, one greatest signal automatically promoted. verbose Logical. TRUE, function print messages indicating progress process. Note , context generate_sector_constraints, benchmark_weights_m_d_ref data frame must also supplied.

title: Geometric Mean Return (percentage-point scale) — geometric_mean_return
text: Computes geometric mean vector returns expressed percentage points (e.g. 2 2 %, -0.75 -0.75 %). Internally function converts input decimal form, performs compounding, returns result percentage-point scale.

title: Geometric Mean Return (percentage-point scale) — geometric_mean_return

title: Geometric Mean Return (percentage-point scale) — geometric_mean_return
text: returns Numeric vector returns. return must greater -100 (.e. return worse -100 %). na.rm Logical. NA values removed?  Default FALSE. mult_last_n = Integer >= 0. > 0, multiply last n (time order) number. mult_by Numeric scalar. number multiply last n values . Default 0 (skip). scale Numeric divisor/multiplier used convert percentage points decimals.  Default 100.

title: Geometric Mean Return (percentage-point scale) — geometric_mean_return
text: single numeric value (percentage points) representing compounded average return.

title: Geometric Mean Return (percentage-point scale) — geometric_mean_return
text: computation $$\left(\prod_{=1}^{n}\left(1 + \frac{r_i}{\text{scale}}\right)\right)^{1/n} - 1$$ converted back percentage-point scale multiplying decimal result scale.

title: Geometric Mean Return (percentage-point scale) — geometric_mean_return
code: # 2 % , 0.5 % and -1 % expressed as 2, 0.5, -1 geometric_mean_return(c(2, 0.5, -1))        # ≈ 0.498 (% points) #> [1] 0.4925368 geometric_mean_return(c(2, NA, 1), na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 1.498768

title: Retrieve Processed Feature Positions — get_features_positions
text: base_sb_backtest_results_list list base backtest result objects, may contain chosen signals positions. Passed internal function get_and_check_chosen_signals_and_positions retrieve reference set signals/positions. features_passthrough character vector dictating features retained final output (subsetting reference set). "", features remain; "none", returns "none"; otherwise, must subset features found reference set. features_m_df meta-data object (e.g., meta_dataframe) containing least three key columns (often date, symbol, target) plus feature columns. Used primarily consistency checks reconstructing missing signals/positions base_sb_backtest_results_list.

title: Retrieve Processed Feature Positions — get_features_positions

title: Retrieve Processed Feature Positions — get_features_positions
text: Reference Signals: function first calls get_and_check_chosen_signals_and_positions ensure base backtest elements share identical named positions. Missing elements reconstructed -"long". Feature Filtering: Depending features_passthrough, reference set either left intact (""), discarded ("none"), subset specified feature names. Force Handling: positions labeled "force" treated "long" final output.

title: Define the hrp_parameters S4 Class — hrp_parameters-class
text: linkage character indicating linkage method used hierarchical clustering algorithm. Must one 'single', 'complete', 'average', 'ward.D', 'ward.D2', 'mcquitty', 'median' 'centroid'. exp_ret_score_tilt character indicating apply expected return score tilt. Possible options 'none', 'inner' 'final'. 'none' means tilt applied, 'inner' means tilt applied within risk parity algorithm 'final' means tilt applied risk parity solution computed. exp_ret_score_tilt_eta numeric value representing intensity expected return score tilt. Must positive value. needed exp_ret_score_tilt 'inner' 'final'.

code: hyper_tune(   tuning_method,   ml_algorithm,   target_fwd_name,   full_data_training_sample_clean,   features_validation_sample,   target_validation_sample,   eval_function,   custom_objective_translated,   chosen_eval_metric_translated,   early_stop,   chosen_eval_metric,   huber_delta,   quantile_tau,   hyper_grid_domain_list,   n_iter,   init_points,   k_iter,   acq,   keras_architecture_parameters,   parallel,   verbose )

title: Perform Hyperparameter Tuning for Machine Learning Models — hyper_tune
text: tuning_method character string specifying tuning method. Possible values "random_search", "grid_search", "bayesian_opt". ml_algorithm character string indicating machine learning algorithm used. target_fwd_name character string specifying name target variable forward prediction. full_data_training_sample_clean data frame containing clean training sample data. features_validation_sample data frame feature values validation. target_validation_sample data frame target values validation. eval_function function computes evaluation metric given hyperparameters. custom_objective_translated optional custom objective function used tuning. chosen_eval_metric_translated evaluation metric used internally model performance assessment. early_stop integer specifying number iterations improvement stopping early. chosen_eval_metric character string specifying evaluation metric used. huber_delta numeric value Huber loss delta parameter, applicable using Huber loss. quantile_tau numeric value quantile parameter, applicable using quantile loss. hyper_grid_domain_list list specifying domain hyperparameters search . n_iter integer specifying number iterations grid random search Bayesian optimization. init_points integer specifying number initial random points Bayesian optimization. k_iter integer specifying number iterations scoring function sampling Bayesian optimization. acq character string specifying acquisition function Bayesian optimization. keras_architecture_parameters list parameters specifying architecture Keras model. parallel logical indicating whether evaluate candidates parallel (future backend). verbose logical indicating whether print timing progress information.

title: Perform Hyperparameter Tuning for Machine Learning Models — hyper_tune
text: list containing: chosen_eval_metric_validation_current_date data frame evaluation metrics set hyperparameters tried. optimal_hyper named vector optimal hyperparameter values found. validation_eval_metrics_hyper_choice_current_date named vector evaluation metrics corresponding optimal hyperparameter set.

title: Map a Recipe to Sequential Dates — map_recipe_timewise
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Build a per-date recipe: identifier columns as id_vars, then impute + winsorize predictors rec <- recipes::recipe(panel@data) %>%   recipes::update_role(id, tickers, dates, new_role = "id_vars") %>%   recipes::update_role(recipes::all_numeric(), new_role = "predictor") %>%   recipes::step_impute_median(recipes::all_numeric_predictors()) %>%   step_winsorize(recipes::all_numeric_predictors(), probs = c(0.05, 0.95))  silver <- map_recipe_timewise(panel, rec, parallel = FALSE, type = "generic") } # }

Storing raw table (data) reproducible workflow history enables reproducible pipelines safe application subsequent transforms. Use package constructor (e.g. create_meta_dataframe()) available; populate slots run validation. low-level S4 constructor new("meta_dataframe", ...) may used testing must satisfy validation rules.

title: meta_dataframe-class — meta_dataframe-class
text: data data.frame containing actual tabular observations. Required first columns: id, tickers, dates. workflow list (typically sb_backtest_workflow object plain list) recording sequence preprocessing / modeling steps applied data. element describe one operation (name, parameters, timestamp, etc.). signals character vector names columns data considered signals (features, targets, groupings, etc.). unique_dates Numeric: number distinct dates present data. unique_tickers Numeric: number distinct tickers present data. n_obs Numeric: total number observations (rows) data. current_date Date object representing reference/current date object. meta_dataframe_name short character identifier meta_dataframe instance.

title: meta_dataframe-class — meta_dataframe-class
text: underlying data must data.frame. first three columns data must exactly id, tickers, dates order. tickers must character vector; dates must class Date; id must character equal paste0(tickers, "-", dates). Rows must ordered id (alphabetical order) dates must non-decreasing. Column names (variable names) must unique must include prefix "low_" signals (prefix breaks certain backtesting routines). validity function also calls is_coercible_to_meta_dataframe() prints helpful messages coercion checks fail.

title: meta_dataframe-class — meta_dataframe-class
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Assume create_meta_dataframe() is the user-facing constructor df <- data.frame(   id = c("A-2020-01-01", "B-2020-01-02"),   tickers = c("A", "B"),   dates = as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2020-01-02")),   signal1 = c(0.1, -0.2),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  mdf <- create_meta_dataframe(   data = df,   workflow = list(),   signals = c("signal1"),   meta_dataframe_name = "example_mdf" )  # low-level creation (not recommended unless you ensure validation) new_mdf <- new(   "meta_dataframe",   data = df,   workflow = list(),   signals = "signal1",   unique_dates = length(unique(df$dates)),   unique_tickers = length(unique(df$tickers)),   n_obs = nrow(df),   current_date = Sys.Date(),   meta_dataframe_name = "example_lowlevel" ) } # }

what: An S4 Class Storing a Time Series Plus Metadata — meta_xts-class
title: An S4 Class Storing a Time Series Plus Metadata — meta_xts-class
text: Base container class xts time series together metadata needed track provenance shape. normally instantiated directly; use create_meta_xts(), builds appropriate subclass (returns_meta_xts-class metrics_meta_xts-class) .

title: An S4 Class Storing a Time Series Plus Metadata — meta_xts-class
text: data xts object containing time series. meta_xts_name Character. label ID series. metric_name Character. Name metric/return series stored. workflow . placeholder user-defined workflow/pipeline objects (e.g. log processing steps produced data). n_dates Numeric. Number rows data. source Character. Origin column, one entry per column data. frequency Character. Detected time frequency (e.g. "daily", "weekly", "monthly", "yearly"). current_date Date. recent date data.

title: An S4 Class Storing a Time Series Plus Metadata — meta_xts-class
text: Dates data must strictly increasing (duplicated timestamps). n_dates must equal nrow(data). source must one entry per column data. Column names data must duplicated.

what: An S4 Subclass of meta_xts for Metric Series (Holes Allowed) — metrics_meta_xts-class
title: An S4 Subclass of meta_xts for Metric Series (Holes Allowed) — metrics_meta_xts-class
text: Stores arbitrary metric time series (e.g. factor exposures, signals) required gap-free, unlike returns_meta_xts-class.

title: An S4 Subclass of meta_xts for Metric Series (Holes Allowed) — metrics_meta_xts-class
text: series Character. Column names data (one per metric). n_series Numeric. Number metric columns.

title: An S4 Subclass of meta_xts for Metric Series (Holes Allowed) — metrics_meta_xts-class
text: Inherits checks meta_xts-class. n_series must equal ncol(data), series must match colnames(data). check date consecutiveness – holes (e.g. illiquid tickers metrics undefined every date) expected allowed.

what: Compute the Minimum Track Record Length — min_track_record
title: Compute the Minimum Track Record Length — min_track_record
text: Calculates minimum number observations required observed Sharpe Ratio statistically greater reference Sharpe Ratio given confidence level, adjusting skewness kurtosis.

title: Compute the Minimum Track Record Length — min_track_record
code: min_track_record(x)

title: Compute the Minimum Track Record Length — min_track_record
text: x numeric vector returns (decimal form).

title: Compute the Minimum Track Record Length — min_track_record
text: named list : min_trl Minimum track record length required. is_significant Logical value: current Sharpe Ratio statistically significant? num_of_extra_obs_needed Number additional observations needed (0 already significant).

title: Compute the Minimum Track Record Length — min_track_record
text: implementation follows methodology Bailey Lopez de Prado (2012), assumes returns maintain similar statistical properties --sample.

text: mmaf_method character indicating MMAF method used. Must one 'top_down' 'bottom_up'. top_down_proxy_port_method character indicating method used constructing top-proxy portfolio. Must one 'ew', 'rp', 'hrp', 'cs' 'sw' mmaf_method 'top_down' NULL mmaf_method 'bottom_up'. mmaf_group_col character string representing MMAF group assets belong. used group assets constructing micro macro portfolios. must length 1 character. micro_port_config object class mmaf_sub_port_config representing configuration constructing micro portfolios. macro_port_config object class mmaf_sub_port_config representing configuration constructing macro portfolios.

text: port_construction_method character string indicating method used constructing micro portfolios. mvo_parameters object class mvo_parameters representing parameters mean-variance optimization. relevant 'mvo'. rp_parameters object class rp_parameters representing parameters risk parity. relevant 'rp'. hrp_parameters object class hrp_parameters representing parameters hierarchical risk parity. relevant 'hrp'.

what: Define the mvo_parameters S4 Class — mvo_parameters-class
title: Define the mvo_parameters S4 Class — mvo_parameters-class
text: S4 class represent set configurations mean-variance optimization.

title: Define the mvo_parameters S4 Class — mvo_parameters-class
text: S4 object class port_backtest_config.

title: Define the mvo_parameters S4 Class — mvo_parameters-class
text: opt_method character indicating optimization method. current available method 'random'. case, n_random_portfolios generated constraints defined mvo_parameters object one optimizes opt_objective selected. random_ports_method character string representing method passed PortfolioAnalytics::random_portfolios generate random portfolios. Options 'sample', 'simplex 'grid'. n_random_ports Number random portfolios generate. needed opt_method 'random'. opt_objective character indicating optimization objective. Possible options 'return', 'risk' 'sharpe'. ridge_pen numeric value representing ridge penalty applied weights. NULL, ridge penalty applied. n_resamples Number resamples perform estimating optimal portfolio. 0, resampling performed. exp_ret_score_jitter numeric value representing standard deviation Gaussian noise added expected returns scores estimating optimal portfolio. used introduce randomness optimization process avoid overfitting expected returns. cov_eigval_jitter numeric value representing standard deviation Gaussian noise added covariance matrix eigenvalues estimating optimal portfolio. used introduce randomness optimization process avoid overfitting covariance matrix.

title: oos_sb_outputs_m_df-class — oos_sb_outputs_m_df-class
text: oos_sb_outputs_m_df extends meta_dataframe-class represent --sample predictions produced signal-blending backtest pipeline (run_sb_backtest()). stores target values, model predictions associated errors together usual workflow metadata.

title: oos_sb_outputs_m_df-class — oos_sb_outputs_m_df-class
text: --sample Signal-Blending backtest outputs container (S4). class canonical return type --sample evaluation routines signal-blending workflow. Keeping target, pred, error aligned validated enables downstream aggregation, performance reporting diagnostics rely consistent semantics.

title: oos_sb_outputs_m_df-class — oos_sb_outputs_m_df-class
text: sb_backtest_workflow . record (usually list) describing --sample backtest workflow parameters used produce outputs. data data.frame containing observations (first three columns: id, tickers, dates). workflow . Record preprocessing/backtest workflow produced object. signals character. Names columns data represent signals/metrics. unique_dates numeric. Number distinct dates data. unique_tickers numeric. Number distinct tickers data. n_obs numeric. Total number observations (rows) data. current_date Date. Reference/current date object. meta_dataframe_name character. Short identifier meta_dataframe instance.

title: oos_sb_outputs_m_df-class — oos_sb_outputs_m_df-class
text: Objects must satisfy parent-class checks plus: data must include columns id, tickers, dates, target, pred, error. rows target NA, error must equal target - pred. rows target pred NA, error must NA.

title: Plot meta_dataframe — plot-meta_dataframe
text: S4 plot method meta_dataframe objects. Produces variety diagnostic summary visualisations (time series, cross-sectional, histogram, boxplot, composition, regression, density2d, correlogram, radar, waterfall, tile heatmap, frequency). function performs internal filtering summarisation rendering ggplot (base plot radar charts).

title: Plot meta_dataframe — plot-meta_dataframe
code: # S4 method for class 'meta_dataframe,missing' plot(   x,   type = NULL,   clustering_variables = "auto",   variable = NULL,   tickers = NULL,   dates = NULL,   calc_stat = NULL,   custom_filter = NULL,   filter_values = NULL,   dep_y = NULL,   numeric_aggregation = "decile",   palette = "cyberpunk" )

title: Plot meta_dataframe — plot-meta_dataframe
text: x meta_dataframe object. type Character. Plot type. One "cross_sectional", "time_series", "histogram", "boxplot", "composition", "regression", "density2d", "correlogram", "radar", "waterfall", "tile_heatmap", "frequency". clustering_variables Character vector "auto". Columns group/facet (e.g. "dates", "tickers"). Use NULL clustering. variable Character vector. Main numeric variable(s) analyse visualise (must column names x@data). bivariate plots, supply two variables appropriate. tickers Character vector "". Tickes include (default ""). dates Either single Date, vector Date, date range, "". calc_stat Character. Summary statistic metric compute (e.g. "mean", "sd", "median", "cor", "beta", "alpha", quantiles like "q05", etc.). Defaults vary type. custom_filter Character character vector. Column(s) filter (e.g. "sector"). filter_values vector list values used filter custom_filter columns. dep_y Optional character. Dependent variable used bivariate statistics (required calc_stat "cor", "beta", "alpha" type "regression"). numeric_aggregation Character. discretize numeric clustering variables: one "decile", "quartile", "tercile", "median". Default: "decile". palette Character. Colour palette use plot (e.g. "cyberpunk", "br").

title: Plot meta_dataframe — plot-meta_dataframe
text: ggplot object (base plot radar). plot printed side-effect.

title: Plot meta_dataframe — plot-meta_dataframe
text: Plot method meta_dataframe objects Univariate plots use variable. Bivariate plots require dep_y two variables. tile_heatmap composition perform internal quantile binning aggregation. clustering_variables="auto" function interactively prompt sensible defaults run interactive session. Many arguments can selected interactively omitted. Required plotting packages: ggplot2, gridExtra, scales, ggdist, ggraph, ggrepel, igraph, RColorBrewer. method stops dependencies missing.

title: Plot meta_dataframe — plot-meta_dataframe
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Cross-sectional mean of 'score' by tickers on a given date plot(mdf, type = "cross_sectional", variable = "score", calc_stat = "mean",      clustering_variables = "tickers", dates = as.Date("2026-07-01"))  # Time series of 'momentum' for a specific ticker plot(mdf, type = "time_series", variable = "momentum",      clustering_variables = "dates", tickers = "AAPL")  # Histogram of a signal distribution plot(mdf, type = "histogram", variable = "signal", clustering_variables = "tickers")  # Boxplot of a signal across tickers plot(mdf, type = "boxplot", variable = "signal", clustering_variables = "tickers")  # Composition of themes (uses calc_stat = "mean") plot(mdf, type = "composition", variable = "theme")  # Regression of signal_x against return_1m (bivariate) plot(mdf, type = "regression", variable = "signal_x", dep_y = "return_1m",      clustering_variables = "tickers")  # 2D density of two signals plot(mdf, type = "density2d", variable = c("signal_x", "signal_y"), tickers = "all")  # Correlogram across several metrics plot(mdf, type = "correlogram", variable = c("signal_a", "signal_b", "signal_c"))  # Radar chart (requires >= 3 variables) plot(mdf, type = "radar", variable = c("IR", "alpha", "turnover"))  # Waterfall (e.g., cumulative contribution) plot(mdf, type = "waterfall", variable = "exp_ret", calc_stat = "sum",      clustering_variables = "tickers")  # Tile heatmap over dates × groups (quantile-binned) plot(mdf, type = "tile_heatmap", variable = "score", clustering_variables = c("dates", "tickers"))  # Frequency plot for categorical/count diagnostics plot(mdf, type = "frequency", variable = "sector") } # }

title: Plot Method for 'port' Objects — plot,port,missing-method
text: ggplot object (invisibly). function also prints plot.

title: Plot Method for 'port' Objects — plot,port,missing-method
text: currently supported plot types : "weights" - bar chart portfolio weights (active weights). "exp_ret_score" - bar chart portfolio's expected return scores. "risk_return" - scatterplot expected return score (y) vs. risk (x). "correlation" - heatmap asset correlation matrix. "relative_risk_contribution" - bar chart comparing weights risk contributions. "efficient_frontier" - scatterplot random portfolios Sharpe ratio coloring optimal portfolio highlighted. "random_weights_distribution" - jitter plot random portfolio weights constraints. "group_composition" - grouped bar chart comparing portfolio benchmark weights across classification variables (e.g., sectors). "hierarchical_clustering" - dendrogram showing hierarchical clustering assets. method dispatched signature plot(x = "port", y = "missing") use y argument.

text: x object class sb_backtest_results containing results walk-forward validation. plot_id character string numeric value specifying plot display. Available plots depend sb_algorithm. ML algorithms ("glmnet", "rf", "xgb", "nn"): "Chosen Evaluation Metric Time" "Test vs Validation Chosen Evaluation Metric Time" "Best Hyperparameters Time" "Hyperparameters vs Error" "Evaluation Metrics Time" "Consolidated OOS Testing Metrics" "OOS Predictions, Errors Targets" "Consolidated OOS Testing Metrics vs Average Validation" "Final Signal-Blending Model" "Time-Series Feature Importance Signal" "Average Time-Series Feature Importance Theme" "Compare Feature Importance Side--Side Signal" "Compare Feature Importance Side--Side Theme" "Feature Importance Box-Plot Signal" "Feature Importance Box-Plot Theme" "Feature Importance Heatmap Signal" "Feature Importance Heatmap Theme" "Explain Prediction" "ols" heuristic/portfolio algorithms, tuning-specific plots (chosen-metric, test-vs-validation, hyperparameter, consolidated-vs-validation plots) omitted. Provide number (index list shown plot_id NULL), NULL (default) list . features_m_df meta_dataframe containing features used backtest. Required plots like "Explain Prediction". palette Character. what: Portfolio classes for backtesting portfolios — port-class

text: classes encapsulate various parameters constraints used backtesting portfolios.

title: Portfolio classes for backtesting portfolios — port-class
text: universe_m_d_ref meta_dataframe containing universe assets, weights per-asset metrics attached. port_construction_method character string specifying method used construct portfolio. Must one c("ew", "sw", "cw", "cs", "rp", "hrp", "mvo", "mmaf", "custom_weights"). eligible_assets character vector eligible assets portfolio. exp_ret_score eligible assets expected return scores. Required port_construction_method "sw", "cs" "mvo", also "rp", "hrp" "mmaf" exp_ret_score column present universe_m_d_ref (e.g. risk-parity tilt). covariance_matrix eligible assets covariance matrix returns. Must rownames identical colnames symmetric. correlation_matrix object storing correlation matrix returns (optional covariance provided). weights numeric vector portfolio weights eligible assets. rel_risk_contr object representing relative risk contributions (must provided covariance_matrix NULL). clusters object storing clusters assets (used HRP). mvo_port_spec object class portfolio.spec (PortfolioAnalytics similar package) used Markowitz optimization. ind_max_weights numeric vector specifying maximum weight constraints per asset. ind_min_weights numeric vector specifying minimum weight constraints per asset. random_port_weights object storing random portfolio weights (used MVO). groups object grouping assets (e.g., sectors). group_col character naming group column groups used macro/group aggregation (used MMAF group-level analytics). mmaf_method object storing MMAF method (used MMAF). group_cov_matrix object storing group covariance matrix (used MMAF). micro object storing micro-level portfolio (used MMAF). macro object storing macro-level portfolio (used MMAF). selected_benchmark_port object storing selected benchmark portfolio. port_stats one-row data.frame portfolio (, applicable, group active/benchmark-relative) analytics portfolio. port_name character giving unique name label portfolio.
